title: VP of Software Engineering
description:
VP of Software Engineering
North America, Remote
Apply

About CoLab

At CoLab, we help mechanical engineering teams bring life-changing products to market years sooner.

CoLab is the AI platform for driving stronger engineering decisions. Every design review in CoLab builds a knowledge repository of design feedback, decisions, and lessons learned - which AI agents draw from to flag issues on future designs before they compound. The more your team works in CoLab, the smarter it gets and the faster you arrive at the ideal design. Companies like Ford, Komatsu, and Johnson Controls use CoLab to catch issues earlier, eliminate rework cycles, and bring products to market faster.

Founded in St. John’s, Newfoundland, CoLab has grown quickly from our first customer in 2019 to a rapidly scaling company. We’ve recently been recognized on Deloitte’s Fast 50™ and Fast 500™, and named a Canadian company to watch by The Globe and Mail and Financial Post. 

About the Role

This role is for a high-impact engineering leader who has been in the trenches at fast-scaling startups — someone who has grown from individual contributor to leader and helped scale engineering orgs through multiple phases of growth. Ideally, you've done it more than once.

As VP of Software Engineering, you'll be responsible for building and maintaining a high-performing, scalable engineering organization across Product Development, Ops, and Technical Support. This is a role for someone who’s hands-on enough to spot an

Embark on a career with an IT company in business since 1985 and committed to providing advanced tools and systems for global organizations! 

Compusult Limited is a dynamic and growing company headquartered in Mount Pearl, NL with Canadian branch offices in Halifax, NS; a U.S. subsidiary in Chantilly, VA; and a European office located in The Hague, Netherlands. We provide state-of-the-art software and systems for vital, mission-impacting technical environments, as well as associated services and support, to government and commercial entities, in particular the aerospace and defence industries. Compusult also designs and manufactures our own line of Unmanned Ground Vehicles (UGVs) and Assistive Technology products.

Compusult hires employees and subcontractors based primarily on required education and experience, and offers the same compensation to individuals and companies at the same levels of experience.

Compusult is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to treating all our employees and job applicants equally. Compusult avoids unlawful discrimination in all aspects of employment, including recruitment and selection, promotion, transfer, opportunities for training, pay and benefits, other terms of employment, discipline, selection for redundancy and dismissal.

Granville Biomedical is a Canadian health tech company dedicated to advancing women’s pelvic and reproductive health through the design and commercialization of innovative anatomical models and educational tools. Our mission is to empower clinicians, educators, and patients globally with products that promote health equity, evidence-based care, and inclusive design.

Over the past year, we have made significant progress by expanding internationally, launching new proprietary products, and building strategic partnerships in women’s health. We are now seeking a highly motivated and self-driven Women's Health Sales & Business Development Lead to help amplify our global impact and take our growth to the next level.

The ideal candidate will hail from a sales or account manager background with an interest in marketing and experience working within a collaborative team environment. title: Senior Solutions Architect, Government Partnerships
description: About UsPropel is on a mission to empower low-income Americans by simplifying access to government benefits with modern technology. We're a passionate team of 100 Propellers - all working to help our users get through the month, every month.About the RoleWe're looking for a Solutions Architect to bridge Propel's Government Partnerships team and our Product & Engineering organization - translating state partners' needs into product strategy, and then owning the delivery of that work with states. This role has the opportunity to shape the way our product evolves with deeper government partnerships and increase the value we’re able to deliver to both users and government partners.This is a hands-on IC role. You'll be the person writing the scoping doc, building the dashboard, sitting in the state meeting, configuring the campaign, and running the evaluation. You own both strategy and execution end to end. Over time, as this work scales, the role has potential to grow into a leadership position — but today we need someone who is excited about the execution.You'll work across the state partnership funnel, from early relationship-building through campaign delivery. You should be the kind of person who can walk into a meeting with a state SNAP agency, understand their operational pain points around payment error rates or portal access, walk out with a concrete plan that maps to Propel's product roadmap — and then go execute it. SYSTEMS ENGINEER - C-CORE

At C-CORE, we are committed to investing in people and creating a dynamic and rewarding employment experience. We are seeking a Systems Engineer (CE, EE or Mechatronics background preferred) with a strong background in embedded systems, software development, communications and testing experience to work with our highly skilled team of engineers and scientists. If you are self-motivated, a team player, and committed to technical excellence, then C-CORE could be the right environment for you.

About C-CORE

C-CORE conducts R&D and provides research-based advisory services, delivering innovative engineering and technology solutions to national and international clients. Over the past 50 years, we have built a world-class team of 70 dedicated professionals with a reputation for excellence in Remote Sensing. We are looking for outstanding engineers and scientists to join our team.

Position Overview

This position requires an engineering degree with experience in CubeSat development, particularly in systems engineering tasks related to payload development, integration, testing and verification.

The successful candidate will have exceptional analysis and problem-solving skills, strong communication skills (spoken and written) and proven success working in a team environment.

title: Saint John Litigation Lawyer
description: Are you an experienced litigator with a passion for law and a desire to contribute to diverse, impactful legal matters? Do you want to be part of a dynamic and growing firm that values both personal and professional development?McInnes Cooper is currently seeking an experienced Litigation Lawyer to join our thriving team in Saint John, New Brunswick, where you will have the opportunity to engage in a broad range of complex litigation matters. Our litigation team provides highly diversified services to clients across various industries, regularly appearing in courts at all levels in the Atlantic Provinces, as well as in the Federal Court and the Supreme Court of Canada. Additionally, the team frequently represents clients in administrative and regulatory tribunals, arbitrations, and mediations.As a Litigation Associate, your key responsibilities will include:Managing your own files while collaborating with colleagues on complex legal matters.Providing legal services across a wide array of areas, such as general/civil litigation, commercial litigation, estate litigation, insurance defense, and construction/engineering litigation.Conducting legal research, providing legal opinions, and maintaining up-to-date knowledge on relevant legal developments.Communicating effectively with clients, colleagues, and opposing counsel to ensure successful outcomes.Representing clients in court, at hearings, and in negotiations.The successful candidate will possess the following qualifications:

title: Academic-Industry Partnerships, Advisor - Queen’s University
description:
Do you want to be part of a mission driven organization shaping Canada’s innovation future? Do you thrive in collaborative environments where creativity, continuous improvement, and strategic thinking are valued? Are you motivated by work that strengthens Canada’s competitiveness and delivers meaningful impact for communities and businesses? 
 
At Mitacs, we take a talent first approach to building Canada’s innovation capacity. We connect researchers with organizations across every region of the country to help solve pressing challenges and accelerate economic growth. By enabling business academic collaboration and supporting the development of highly skilled talent, we contribute to a resilient, future ready Canadian economy. 
 
Guided by our values — Impact, Integrity, Collaboration, Excellence, and Talent — we work with partners nationwide to advance research, commercialization, and innovation. 
 
At Mitacs, you become part of a team committed to empowering Canadian talent, fostering meaningful partnerships, and driving innovation that supports Canada’s long-term competitiveness. If you’re inspired by big ideas and passionate about creating national impact, we encourage you to apply.

Do you want to be part of an innovative and impact-driven team? 
Do you enjoy working collaboratively with others? Are you someone who brings curiosity, creativity, and a continuous-improvement mindset to your work? Do you take a strategic approach to your work? Do you enjoy working in a dynamic culture where your perspectives are valued and respected? Do you enjoy building partnerships to address shared challenges and deliver meaningful outcomes?
 
Mitacs is a national not-for-profit that helps industry and non-profit partners solve strategic challenges by leveraging Canada’s world-class post-secondary talent and a global network of industry, academic, and government partnerships. Through these collaborations, Mitacs helps business and community partners succeed, improves students’ on-the-job skills development, and strengthens Canada’s world-class innovation ecosystem. At Mitacs, we strive to develop the next generation of innovators with vital research and business skills through learning, collaboration, and meaningful impact. In partnership with domestic and international companies, governments, and academia, we support a new economy using Canada’s most valuable resource – its people.

Provide general administrative support, scheduling meetings, and maintaining records.

title: Senior Platform Engineer
description: About UsPropel is on a mission to build technology that strengthens the social safety net. We’re a passionate team of ~100 Propellers who envision a future where every American has the tools and resources they need to thrive.The RoleAre you a Platform Engineer looking to make a big impact on a mission-driven company? Do you enjoy architecting software that shapes an entire engineering organization? Do you thrive in high-autonomy startup environments?We’re looking for a self-motivated Senior Platform Engineer to join our Developer Enablement Team within the engineering org at Propel.This role empowers other engineers by improving the cloud native platforms that enable development and delivery of Propel’s technology. These cloud native platforms impact several products and stacks, including interacting with government programs, surfacing savings opportunities, enrolling users in healthcare plans, internal tooling, architecture, and more! Your focus will be on running fault tolerant applications in Kubernetes and AWS, and solving organization-wide engineering problems. We are looking for a tinkerer to experiment with improvements to the developer experience at Propel. You will architect new features for our cloud platform, support a wide-variety of production applications, address technical debt, and help guide other engineers.What we offer:An engineering culture that is humble, motivated, and collaborative Consistent knowledge sharing through projects or bugs demos, Lunch & Lea

ABOUT US
 
RESOURCES

SENIOR PROGRAMMER

Do you dream of having a say in the software you write? Strong Data Automation is an old company with a start up mentality that currently specializes in waste management logistics. That’s garbage you say? Exactly! We are a tight team working to build essential, usable products for our customers quickly and everyone pitches in to get the job done right. You’ll work on problems of secure data collection, operation scaling and automation, and hardware integrations across multiple applications and geographical locations. Customers will love you for making their job about high level decision making instead of copy and pasting between spreadsheets. We handle tracking, billing, trending and more as we try to revolutionize how organizations control what happens to everything the world doesn’t want.

ROLE REQUIREMENTS
5+ years programming experience
 
Computer science degree or equivalent experience / education
 
Full stack programming experience working primarily in the .NET ecosystem
 
Familiar with modern development best practices and design patterns
 
Knowledge of key security fundamentals for web application development
 
Strong knowledge of SQL Server database
 
Thrives in an atmosphere of taking on new challenges and developing new skills

Customer Service Representative

Have you ever noticed how some jobs don’t feel like work? We do. That’s why we go out of our way to make sure our employees love coming to work every day. For starters, they get to take their birthdays off…with pay. Sound good? There’s a lot more! If you would like to join our dynamic team, we currently have three Customer Service Positions available for our growing team.

The ideal candidate will deliver excellence in customer service when answering the phones for our clients using proper telephone etiquette and will embrace Telelink’s core values. You have the ability to work evening and weekend shifts; strong knowledge of internet navigation; typing speed of 30 wpm would be an asset; strong interpersonal and communication skills and the ability to work effectively with a wide range of constituencies in a diverse company. Knowledge of Microsoft Office applications would be an asset.

Working With Telelink

Telelink is a 60+ year old start-up. Our history is in the traditional answering service industry. Our future is one that bridges humans and technology to deliver connected customer experiences.  

As the Sales Executive, you are the first impression, the problem solver, and the gas that fuels our revenue engine. Your primary responsibilities include qualifying inbound leads, progressing qualified opportunities all the way through contract negotiations using a consultative selling approach, hunting for strategic customers in our target markets, and advising potential customers on the right solutions that deliver their desired outcomes. As the first point of contact on the customer journey, you set the tone for Telelink’s customer experience by delving into the customer’s desired outcomes, and ensuring these and all details required to deliver on those outcomes are fully documented and cascaded to other teams along the customer journey.

title: Senior/Experienced Product Designer
description:
What we’re doingWe’re building the AI powered coaching platform that transforms how gamers learn, compete, and win. Backed by a16z, we’re already live in 5 titles with 100K+ users and rapidly expanding into new games, hardware partnerships, and SDK integrations. 
The design team plays a significant role at trophi.ai. We shape how players first understand and feel the product, from the very first minute in the game through the loops that bring them back. We turn ambiguity into clear, intuitive experiences, use research and data to guide decisions, and partner closely with product, engineering, and data science to ship quickly. As a senior designer you will set the quality bar, own our systems and patterns, and define the look and feel of an AI powered coach that meets players inside their favorite games and our studio customers.

Things you’ll do

Lead end-to-end product design from discovery and flows to polished prototypes and user testing.
Turn ambiguous ideas into cohesive UX that drives core business outcomes around activation, retention and onboarding (First session clarity, engagement loops etc)
Partner with product and data science to run lightweight usability testing, interpret activation and retention funnels, and use insights to iterate quickly.
Obsess about impact, first on onboarding, first session clarity, and engagement loops.
Build and own our design system for speed and consistency across surfaces
Work with product, engineering, and data science to ship high q

title: Partnership Strategy Manager
description:
What we’re doingtrophi.ai is the AI powered coaching platform that transforms how gamers learn, compete, and win. Backed by a16z, with $12M in funding, we’re already live in five titles with 100K+ users and rapidly expanding into new games, hardware partnerships, and SDK integrations.
Our next stage of growth is about building the right ecosystem around trophi.ai from studios and publishers to hardware makers and platforms. We’re looking for a Partnerships Lead reporting to the head of product & partnerships who can hunt, structure, and close deals that unlock distribution, revenue, and product opportunities.

Things you’ll do

Build and own a pipeline of studios and publishers for our B2B product line focused on personalized onboarding.
Contribute to our ecosystem and market entry strategy, mapping opportunities and frameworks with the Head of Product.
Hunt and close new ecosystem partnerships (hardware, platforms, service providers) that generate revenue and elevate the player experience.
Manage and expand existing strategic partnerships (e.g., Fanatec, Corsair, Grid & Go), ensuring smooth delivery, reporting, and upsell opportunities.
Assist in Scoping, pitch, and deliver proof of concepts (POCs) with partners, then convert them into scaled commercial agreements.
Develop and deliver proposals, presentations, and partner materials that resonate with decision-makers.
Collaborate with Product and Data Science to scope integrations and deliver on partner requirements.
Represen

title: Strategy & Operations Associate
description:
What we’re doingWe’re building the AI-powered coaching platform that transforms how gamers learn, compete, and win. Backed by a16z with $12M raised, we’re live in 5 titles with 100K+ users and rapidly expanding into new games, hardware partnerships, and SDK integrations.

We’re hiring a Business Operations Associate to join our core team and work directly with the founders to build the backbone of our business. This is a unique opportunity to help scale the world’s first AI coaching platform for gamers at the intersection of gaming and AI.

What You’ll Do

Strategy & Analysis

Partner with the Head of Product & CEO on core strategic initiatives (B2B + B2C).
Lead market analysis, competitive deep dives, and opportunity sizing to identify trends, customer needs, and emerging opportunities.
Build business cases and quantitative models that inform go/no-go decisions on new games, partnerships, and investment bets.
Help chase down ambiguous, zero-to-one problems , from hypothesis to framework to action plan.

Ops & Execution

Build the operating backbone: architect workflows, OKRs, and business review processes that scale with the company.
Drive cross-functional initiatives across Product, Partnerships, and Marketing to ensure alignment and delivery.
Create leverage for leadership by taking point on pitch decks, proposals, and high-stakes projects that require polish and speed.

Growth Enablement

Support partnerships and BD with market research, TAMs, and strategic storytelling.

title: Product Manager
description:
What we’re doingWe’re building the AI-powered coaching platform that transforms how gamers learn, compete, and win. Backed by a16z and $12M in funding, we’re live in 5 titles with 100K+ users, expanding rapidly into new games, hardware partnerships, and SDK integrations.

You’ll Thrive Here If…


You’re energized by ambiguity and move fast to bring clarity where others stall.
You get a rush from finding the simple, elegant solution to a messy, complex problem.
You think like a GM//always asking how a product choice impacts revenue, growth, and user success.
You’re creative at problem solving — able to structure messy problems, build frameworks, and beat ambiguity with clear, actionable paths forward.


Some things you’ll do


User discovery & insights

Embed yourself into gamer communities (Reddit, Discord, conferences) to surface real user needs and trends.
Talk to users, run surveys, and synthesize feedback into actionable insights.
Translate customer problems & business goals into clear, prioritized product requirements.
Analyze product usage patterns (SQL, Mixpanel) and connect insights directly to business outcomes.





Data-driven decision-making

Define success metrics, own dashboards/funnels/experiment readouts, and deliver decision-ready insights.
Size and scope opportunities using product data, market sizing, and an 80/20 lens to de-risk decisions quickly.
Identify opportunities to double down, design/run experiments (A/B tests, pilots), and present clear recommend

title: Intermediate Product Designer
location: St. John's, Newfoundland and Labrador, Canada
description:
What we’re doing
We’re building the AI powered coaching platform that transforms how gamers learn, compete, and win. Backed by a16z, we’re already live in 5 titles with 100K+ users and rapidly expanding into new games and beyond our own app to power coaching for major partners in fitness, wearables, AR/VR, and even aviation. 

The design team plays a significant role at trophi.ai. We don't just build our own interface, we design "plug and play" coaching experiences that live inside our partners' ecosystems.

As a Product Designer, you will work hands-on to turn ambiguity into clear, intuitive experiences. You will partner closely with product and engineering teams to ship features quickly. Instead of managing others, your focus will be on craft: execute high-quality flows that might live in a mobile app, a post-session report, or an overlay in a simulator and designs that help users master complex skills, from landing a plane to perfecting a golf swing.

Things you’ll do

Execute end-to-end design and take projects from requirements and rough sketches to polished prototypes, embeddable UI components and SDKs.
Create cohesive UX flows that drive core business outcomes, specifically focusing on activation, onboarding, and first-session clarity.
Collaborate on research andsupport product and data science teams in running usability tests, interpreting funnels, and using those insights to iterate on your designs.

Careers at Virtual Marine
WHO ARE WE?

Virtual Marine is a privately held Canadian company with its headquarters in Paradise, Newfoundland and Labrador.  Our mission is to improve the safety of life at sea by providing realistic, effective, and safe training tools for small craft operators. Our simulators allow crews of small marine craft to train for real-life situations in environments that are often too dangerous and costly for live boat exercises. 

We’re growing our strong, cohesive, aligned team with a can-do attitude, that’s driven by our values. We foster an inclusive culture and it shows - staff turnover is lower than industry average. We celebrate wins, and take care of each other. 

WHAT DO WE OFFER?

We offer a competitive salary and benefits package and a rewarding challenge with our dynamic and growing team solving big problems!

Interested in seeing some of our work? 
Visit our YouTube channel @VMTechnology or check out the rest of our website or social media.

Project Manager (reposted) 
The Position

As the Project Manager, you will play a lead role in planning, coordinating and overseeing the successful implementation of client-based projects and deliverables. title: Account Executive, ERP Solutions
description:
Account Executive, SAP ERP
Permanent Full Time Location: Remote (open to applicants in the following states: AL, AR, IL, LO, MO, MS, TX)
Grow Your Earnings. Close Lucrative Deals. Build Your Career.

At Vision33, we are looking for a driven Account Executive who is motivated by building relationships, closing net-new business, and significantly growing their income through performance. 

High performers thrive here. With our uncapped commission potential, strong sales support, and a proven delivery organization, Account Executives can substantially and consistently grow their income year over year.


The Role:
As an Account Executive, you will take ownership of the full sales cycle for net-new ERP software opportunities, developing and executing strategies that generate pipeline, win new clients, and exceed revenue targets.
You will:

title: Customer Experience Manager, Cloud Delivery
location: St. John's, Newfoundland and Labrador, Canada
description:
Cloud Experience Manager 
Full Time/Permanent  
Required Location: St. John’s, NL  (Hybrid: Office and Work-from-Home St. John's, NL)

Make our cloud delivery feel seamless, confident, and controlled.  
  
As Customer Experience Manager, you will own the end-to-end client experience for cloud service delivery — from quoting and implementation through go-live and support transition. This role is equal parts coordination, leadership, and customer advocacy – including both delivery leadership and operational ownership. 
 
Headquartered in St. John’s, NL, you’ll lead a delivery team and collaborate across sales, engineering, delivery, and operations to support clients across North America, the United Kingdom, and global locations. 
 
The Role  
You’ll sit at the center of cloud delivery execution, service operations, and customer experience — driving alignment across teams and acting as the primary owner of the delivery experience. Your focus is ensuring cloud solutions are delivered smoothly, meet commercial and operational expectations, and transition successfully into live production with minimal friction.  
 
Key Responsibilities  

Own the end‑to‑end cloud delivery and go‑live experience, ensuring smooth and predictable customer launches.  


Partner with sales to align delivery timelines, scope, and customer expectations.  


Central lead for customer delivery experience, coordinating across sales, engineering, delivery, and operations.

title: Mechanical Engineer
description:
Mechanical Engineer
Canada, Remote
Apply

About CoLab

At CoLab, we help mechanical engineering teams bring life-changing products to market years sooner.
CoLab is the AI platform for driving stronger engineering decisions. Every design review in CoLab builds a knowledge repository of design feedback, decisions, and lessons learned - which AI agents draw from to flag issues on future designs before they compound. The more your team works in CoLab, the smarter it gets and the faster you arrive at the ideal design. Companies like Ford, Komatsu, and Johnson Controls use CoLab to catch issues earlier, eliminate rework cycles, and bring products to market faster.
Founded in St. John’s, Newfoundland, CoLab has grown quickly from our first customer in 2019 to a rapidly scaling company. We’ve recently been recognized on Deloitte’s Fast 50™ and Fast 500™, and named a Canadian company to watch by The Globe and Mail and Financial Post.

About the Role

We’re hiring a Mechanical Engineer to help shape the future of engineering collaboration.

This is not a traditional mechanical engineering role focused on designing physical products. Instead, you’ll work at the intersection of engineering, software, and customer problem-solving — helping ensure CoLab deeply understands how modern engineering teams actually work.

You’ll partner closely with Product, Engineering, Design, Sales, and Customer teams to bring real engineering context into everything we build. Your job is to help bridge the g

At CoLab, we help mechanical engineering teams bring life-changing products to market years sooner.

CoLab is the AI platform for driving stronger engineering decisions. Every design review in CoLab builds a knowledge repository of design feedback, decisions, and lessons learned - which AI agents draw from to flag issues on future designs before they compound. The more your team works in CoLab, the smarter it gets and the faster you arrive at the ideal design. As a Senior Product Manager on the Build Engineering Reviews team at CoLab, you’ll own one of the most strategically important areas of our platform: how engineering teams collaborate, review designs, and make better decisions together.

Partner

Higher Talent is excited to conduct a search for an experienced and driven Partner

for Noseworthy Chapman – Chartered Professional Accountants, located in St. John’s, Newfoundland and Labrador.

WHO WE ARE: 

Noseworthy Chapman is a team of professionals who share an entrepreneurial spirit for success. We’ve chosen to build our careers and raise our families in Newfoundland and Labrador, giving us a grounded, local perspective on what it takes to live, work, and thrive here.

We are the largest independent firm of Chartered Professional Accountants in the province, with a team of more than 50. We pride ourselves on delivering practical, forward-thinking advice to help our clients operate, grow, and succeed in an ever-changing economic landscape. 

Recognized as one of Canada’s Top 100 Small & Medium Employers for ten consecutive years, we maintain a people-first culture grounded in professional development, collaboration, and high performance.

To learn more about us, please visit Noseworthy Chapman.

THE ROLE:

This role offers a unique opportunity to shape the future of a well-established, locally rooted firm while continuing to grow your professional legacy. As a Partner, you will play a key role in client leadership, business development, and firm strategy, while mentoring the next generation of professionals.

title: SAP Business One Production Consultant, Brutos ERP
description:
SAP Business One Production Consultant, Brutos ERP 
Full Time / Permanent Location:Open Canada – East Coast  Language Requirement: English (Preference for English with minimum conversational French) 
 
Ready to combine your SAP Business One expertise with your passion for craft beverages? 

Brutos by Vision33 is growing, and we’re looking for an experienced SAP Business One (Production-focused) Implementation Consultant to help breweries and distilleries streamline operations with our industry-focused ERP solution powered by SAP Business One. 

Brutos by Vision33 is a beverage ERP software that helps brewery and distillery manufacturers increase operational efficiency and gain 360-degree visibility into their business. It is an all-in-one ERP software that harnesses the power of SAP Business One to centralize data by combining front- and back-office functions.  

If you thrive in customer-facing ERP consulting roles and love solving complex production and manufacturing challenges, this could be your next great opportunity. 

Your blend of responsibilities: 

Lead full-cycle SAP Business One implementations, upgrades, and migrations for brewery and distillery customers.  


Guide clients through business process improvements and identify system gaps and optimization opportunities.  


Build strong customer relationships through ongoing consultation and problem-solving.  


Configure solutions, create documentation and reports, and support successful rollouts.  


Collaborate w

title: Business Development Advisor — Southern Alberta - Calgary
description:
Do you want to be part of a mission driven organization shaping Canada’s innovation future? Do you thrive in collaborative environments where creativity, continuous improvement, and strategic thinking are valued? Are you motivated by work that strengthens Canada’s competitiveness and delivers meaningful impact for communities and businesses? 
 
At Mitacs, we take a talent first approach to building Canada’s innovation capacity. We connect researchers with organizations across every region of the country to help solve pressing challenges and accelerate economic growth. By enabling business academic collaboration and supporting the development of highly skilled talent, we contribute to a resilient, future ready Canadian economy. 
 
Guided by our values — Impact, Integrity, Collaboration, Excellence, and Talent — we work with partners nationwide to advance research, commercialization, and innovation. 
 
At Mitacs, you become part of a team committed to empowering Canadian talent, fostering meaningful partnerships, and driving innovation that supports Canada’s long-term competitiveness. If you’re inspired by big ideas and passionate about creating national impact, we encourage you to apply.

title: GTM Strategy & Operations Lead
description: About UsPropel is on a mission to empower low-income Americans by simplifying access to government benefits with modern technology. We're a passionate team of 100 Propellers - all working to help our users get through the month, every month.The RoleWe launched the Propel Marketplace to connect our ~5M monthly active users to products, services, and exclusive offers from leading brands across grocery and retail, healthcare, telecoms, financial services, and auto. It has grown into a powerful, industry-leading performance tool for our partners and a critical revenue driver for Propel. We have strong product market fit, our users love our app, and our customers love that we have a significant reach and engagement with our users.We’re looking for a GTM Strategy and Operations Lead! We're scaling revenue operations for a profitable marketplace - and we need someone who moves fast, thinks strategically, and is eager to provide proactive support to senior leaders.You'll own the RevOps program end-to-end: embedding AI into our tech stack, surfacing insights that drive decisions, and serving as a trusted partner to the Customer Success and Business Development teams. You stay ahead of industry trends and hold yourself to a high bar.This is a high-visibility role on our Marketplace Strategy & Operations team, reporting to the Director of Strategy & Operations. You’ll work closely with the GM of Marketplace, Head of CS, and Head of BD & Sales.Here's how you'll impact the companyPower GT

location: Montreal - 1000 Rue De La Gauchetière Ouest, Canada
description: Putting people first, every day BDO is a firm built on a foundation of positive relationships with our people and our clients. Each day, our professionals provide exceptional service, helping clients with advice and insight they can trust. In turn, we offer an award-winning environment that fosters a people-first culture with a high priority on your personal and professional growth. Your Opportunity BDO Canada is looking for a Senior Manager to join the US Corporate Tax Services team and own the following responsibilities: Provide both technical and project management leadership to team members for US Corporate Tax planning projects or assignments US Corporate Tax planning assignments and projects typically involve providing advice to optimize clients' tax savings in the context of on-off Canada-US cross border transactions, ongoing effective tax rate management, etc. Apply specialized knowledge to anticipate, identify and address issues relevant to clients' businesses Prepare or assist in the preparation of presentation materials and written publications Provide leadership to the group's administrative processes Responsible for tax research (including reviewing research for junior staff) on US issues Engage in training staff and invest in the professional development of your team How do we define success for your role? title: Conseiller sénior ou conseillère séniore, Politiques / Senior Policy Advisor
description:
Vous souhaitez faire partie d’une organisation propulsée par sa mission qui façonne le futur de l’innovation au Canada? Vous aimez évoluer dans des milieux collaboratifs où la créativité, l’amélioration continue et la pensée stratégique sont valorisées? Êtes‑vous une personne motivée par des responsabilités qui contribuent à accroître la compétitivité du Canada et à produire des résultats concrets pour les communautés et les entreprises? 
 
Pour développer la capacité d’innovation du Canada, à Mitacs, nous misons sur le talent. Nous établissons des liens entre les chercheurs et chercheuses et les organisations dans toutes les régions du pays pour aider à résoudre des défis pressants et accélérer la croissance économique. En facilitant la collaboration entre le secteur privé et le milieu postsecondaire et en appuyant le perfectionnement de talents hautement qualifiés, nous contribuons à une économique canadienne résiliente et capable de relever les défis de demain. 
 
Guidé·es par nos valeurs, impact, intégrité, collaboration, excellence et talent, nous collaborons avec des partenaires de partout au pays pour faire avancer la recherche, la commercialisation et l’innovation.

The Opportunity:

At IMP Solutions, we provide IT products, and IT and business consulting services to our clients across Canada. title: Remote Nurse Practitioner
description:
Job Title: Remote Nurse Practitioner
Classification: 1099 Independent Contractor
Work Structure: Fully Remote
Location: United States License Requirements: Colorado, Arizona, or Utah 

About BreatheSuite
BreatheSuite delivers virtual pulmonary rehabilitation to patients with chronic lung conditions across the country. Our mission is to make quality pulmonary rehabilitation accessible, empowering patients to take control of their respiratory health from the comfort of their own homes. We use a multidisciplinary model — NPs, physical therapists, and respiratory therapists — supported by a remote therapeutic monitoring platform built specifically for this population.

The Role
We are looking for an experienced Nurse Practitioner to join our virtual care team. BreatheSuite NPs conduct telehealth evaluations and provide ongoing management for patients across the spectrum of chronic lung conditions — including COPD, ILD, and post-COVID — while overseeing respiratory therapists and collaborating with physical therapists as part of a multidisciplinary care team. This is an opportunity to practice cutting-edge digital health delivery, providing 1-on-1 personalized care entirely via telehealth.

What You'll Do

Conduct comprehensive telehealth evaluations including history, physical examination, and diagnostic review
Develop and manage individualized care plans for patients with chronic lung conditions
Supervise respiratory therapists providing direct patient care
Collaborate with phys

At CoLab, we help mechanical engineering teams bring life-changing products to market years sooner. Our product, CoLab, is the world’s first Design Engagement System (DES) - a category defining product that Engineering teams use to engage in meaningful, productive design conversations, catch preventable mistakes, and get to market faster. Our customers include the largest engineering organizations in the world such as Ford, Johnson Controls, Komatsu, and Polaris in the industrial equipment, consumer products, automotive, aerospace & defense, and shipbuilding industries.

You'll partner closely with Product (who owns in-product experience), Customer Success (who understand customer pain points and workflows), our Implementation Specialist (who delivers hands-on onboarding and sees firsthand where customers struggle), and Marketing (who own brand voice and external content) to build an education infrastructure that supports the entirety of our customer base from first login to advanced use cases.

description:
Senior Product Designer
Canada, Remote

About CoLab

At CoLab, we help mechanical engineering teams bring life-changing products to market years sooner.

CoLab is the AI platform for driving stronger engineering decisions. Every design review in CoLab builds a knowledge repository of design feedback, decisions, and lessons learned - which AI agents draw from to flag issues on future designs before they compound. The more your team works in CoLab, the smarter it gets and the faster you arrive at the ideal design. Companies like Ford, Komatsu, and Johnson Controls use CoLab to catch issues earlier, eliminate rework cycles, and bring products to market faster.

Founded in St. John’s, Newfoundland, CoLab has grown quickly from our first customer in 2019 to a rapidly scaling company. We’ve recently been recognized on Deloitte’s Fast 50™ and Fast 500™, and named  Canadian company to watch by The Globe and Mail and Financial Post. 

About the Role

This isn’t just a design role—it’s a leadership role within our UX design org. As a UX Designer, you’ll be an integral design contributor across our various Build Teams You’ll be responsible for many of the most technically demanding and strategically critical areas of the product.

You’ll go deep on complex workflows, integrations, and system interfaces. Your work will shape how our largest customers interact with CoLab—and set the foundation for how we scale our product across global organizations.

 Job Posts Head Of Engineering / Founding CTO – AI SaaS

HEAD OF ENGINEERING / FOUNDING CTO – AI SAAS

We’re building a local team and want to work as a team together, and to collaborate in person as needed, so this role is for candidates in (or willing to relocate to / regularly commute to) the Halifax area.

About the opportunity:

We’re an early-stage, founder-owned Canadian SaaS startup building an AI-powered social-media marketing and lead-generation platform. The product automates on-brand, compliant content and turns it into qualified leads, a space the current tools don’t serve well.

The founder is looking for the most senior technical person: the owner of architecture and engineering, working shoulder-to-shoulder with the founder to turn the vision into a real, shippable product, and to build the team that ships it. 

This starts as a fractional engagement (about 1 – 2 days/week) and is designed to convert to a full-time Head of Engineering / CTO role as we ramp. We’re looking for a builder-leader, not an advisor.

Key responsibilities:

Technical strategy & architecture: Own the end-to-end architecture (frontend, backend, data, and AI layer) and the major build-vs-buy decisions, e.g., starting on a unified social-posting API and bringing high-volume integrations in-house as we scale.
AI / ML approach: Design a vendor-flexible AI layer, model routing, retrieval (RAG), a lead-qualifyi

SENIOR PRODUCT DESIGNER (UX/UI) AI SAAS – CONTRACT

We’re building a local team and want to work as a team together, and to collaborate in person as needed, so this role is for candidates in (or willing to relocate to / regularly commute to) the Halifax area.

About the opportunity:

We’re an early-stage, founder-owned Canadian SaaS startup building an AI-powered social-media marketing and lead-generation platform. The product automates on-brand, compliant content and turns it into qualified leads, a space the current tools don’t serve well.

We are looking for a Senior Technical Product Designer to shape how the whole thing looks and works, and to give our engineers a clear blueprint to build against.

You’ll work directly with the founder and CTO. This is a contract engagement, front-loaded: intensive for roughly the first 2 – 4 months (≈2 – 4 days/week) building the core, then tapering to part-time to support the build.

What you’ll deliver:

The core user flows and information architecture, how our subscribers/professionals set up their brand, generates content, schedules it, and captures leads.
Wireframes and key screens: profile/persona setup, the content-generation and editor screen, the calendar/scheduler, and the lead-capture pages.

title: Data Operations Specialist
location: St. John's, Newfoundland and Labrador, Canada
description:
Enaimco’s Asset Lifecycle Management (ALM) platform is the place where integrity work happens, embedding daily operations inside a living system of understanding. By connecting physical assets to relevant, searchable digital data, we embed operational workflows directly inside a continuously updating digital twin. This transforms manual data reconciliation into automated clarity, ensuring every engineering decision is based on a current, complete view.

That complete view is just the foundation. By turning asset data into a continuously learning system, our platform delivers contextualized insights today and lays the groundwork for predictive decision-making tomorrow.
We’re looking for a Data Operations Specialist to own how customer data is structured, cleansed, mapped, and validated as it moves into our platform. This is the role that sets the standard for how we handle the messy industrial data our customers bring us, and the discipline that lets every subsequent implementation be faster, more traceable, and more reliable than the last. You’ll be the person who decides how data should be organized for ingestion and ongoing use, then holds the line on those rules across every project.

This position is funded through the Graduate Transition Initiative, delivered by techNL and the Department of Jobs, Growth and Rural Development. The initiative provides support to eligible companies that hire recent graduates into software development and tech sales roles, providing meaningf

Executive Director

ethree Consulting is proud to partner with Campia Gymnastics in their search for a full-time Executive Director.




Who we are

Operating in Mount Pearl, for over 35 years, Campia Gymnastics is a not-for-profit organization dedicated to helping children and youth build strong movement foundations through the sport of gymnastics. Through both competitive and recreational programs, we pride ourselves on creating an inclusive, supportive environment where participants of all ages and abilities feel welcome.




What You’ll Do

Reporting to the Board of Directors, the Executive Director is the senior operational leader of Campia Gymnastics. This pivotal leadership opportunity will provide strategic direction, leadership, operational oversight, and governance for all aspects of Campia; elevating programs, deepening community impact, and positioning Campia for long-term growth and sustainability. You will support our members and stakeholder needs while aligning with Campia’s mission, vision, and strategic objectives.

Manager, Business Liaison Office

Who We Are

Located in Labrador City, Labrador West Chamber of Commerce (LWCC) has been Labrador West’s business and regional economic development voice for more than 50 years. With its strong advocacy efforts and highly successful initiatives, the LWCC has been the voice of our business community. As a non-profit, non-partisan association, we take pride in having the opportunity to continuously play a lead role in representing our respective membership, boosting economic prosperity and quality of life in Labrador City and Wabush.

 

The new Business Liaison Office reflects LWCC’s commitment to supporting its members by serving as a central hub for connection, collaboration, and advocacy - promoting LWCC’s role as the link between businesses and all levels of government to drive growth and development across Labrador. For more information, please visit www.labradorwestchamber.com.

 

The LWCC is seeking a strategic and collaborative leader to serve as Manager of the Business Liaison Office for the region. Our Approach

Stewart McKelvey has a long history of being a leader in Atlantic Canada. And being a leader means embracing innovation.

We changed the traditional approach to law student recruitment in Atlantic Canada. Typically, Articled Clerks wait for “hire-back” decisions well into their articling year.

Stewart McKelvey is thinking differently.

We believe that success in the practice of law requires making a commitment for more than one summer or one year. Our innovative approach removes the traditional “hire-back” decision-making process, typically done at the end of the articling year. A 2L Summer Student will advance to becoming an Articled Clerk, then directly to an Associate at Stewart McKelvey.

Requisition ID: 1779 

Location: Vessels 

Hourly Rate: $41.751   

Application Closing Date: Until Filled  

 

Marine Atlantic is dedicated to creating an environment which promotes employee growth, career development, and job satisfaction.  Our employees embody our core values; they are diverse, safety-oriented team-players who exhibit integrity and commitment, and always strive for excellence.

 

Marine Atlantic is currently looking for a Watchkeeping Engineer (Senior Engineer) to work onboard our vessels that operate from the ports of Port aux Basques NL and North Sydney NS year round; and Argentia NL during the summer season. These positions are permanent full-time spare and relief positions that are scheduled across our fleet and each tour based on operational requirements.  

 

Marine Atlantic is committed to a diverse, equitable and inclusive workplace. Summer Law Student & Articling Opportunities

At McInnes Cooper, we recognize that your professional development is an essential element of becoming a trusted lawyer. Our formal mentorship program will provide you with access to practical hands-on work while exposing you to the business aspects of a legal practice. Our continuing legal education and pro bono programs will further support your development in your legal areas of interest. Although we are considered a larger law firm on the East Coast, we organize ourselves into smaller teams to nurture collaboration and mentorship within our firm. This team model ensures you have the guidance necessary to build a successful legal career.

title: Charlottetown Corporate Paralegal
description:
Are you a legal administrative professional or paraprofessional looking for a new and exciting opportunity? Do you excel in providing exceptional client service while working in a fast-paced, innovative and collaborative environment in which you learn everyday, and have fun doing so?Our Corporate Services team is seeking a Corporate Paralegal to join our Charlottetown office in a full-time permanent position. As part of the Corporate Services team, you will provide support within our corporate department.
Qualifications
The successful candidate will possess the following skill set:The ideal candidate will have a Paralegal or Legal Administration diploma or other related education;·Minimum two (2) years experience as a Legal Assistant or Paralegal preferably within the corporate field;Exceptional technology skills, with proficiency in all aspects of Microsoft Office and legal billing systems;Exceptional communication skills, a special focus on client service and the highest degree of professionalism; and,Experience with knowledge management software and government websites.
Responsibilities
As a Corporate Paralegal, you will be responsible for:Process annual renewals relating to provincial and extra-provincial registrations for which we act as Attorney for Service;Prepare accounts and process payments related to the annual renewal process;Update corporate information at the Prince Edward Island corporate registry;Prepare and update information on annual resolutions/minutes;Pre

company: The Newfoundland and Labrador Construction Association
title: Business Systems Analyst
description:
The Business Systems Analyst is responsible for analysis and definition of business requirements, and the development, implementation, maintenance, and support of business applications, systems and software.

• Define business systems requirements for City divisions and recommend applications / systems / software to meet their needs.
• Implement Commercial Off the Shelf (COTS)/ Software as a Service (SaaS) applications, and develop and deploy custom applications, including user requirements definition, solution design, programming/coding, testing, debugging, training, data conversion, documentation, and deployment, in accordance with Information Technology (IT) Best Practice and established Information Services (IS) departmental procedures.
• Manage application-level user access and security, ensuring appropriate access is maintained for user groups within each application, using varying security administration tools specific to each COTS, SaaS, and custom application.
• Provide primary application support and advisory services to users of the City’s applications and software, including application enhancements, configuration changes, management of software patches / service packs / upgrades, troubleshooting problems and errors, liaising with vendor support resources to identify and report problems and bugs, and determining appropriate resolution to problems.

We are seeking a highly experienced Program Manager (1-year contract) to lead the orchestration and delivery of programs under the Transformation Management Office (TMO) Portfolio, including, but not limited to, Mitacs’ Enterprise Transformation (MET).

Reporting to the Director, Enterprise Transformation, this role is accountable for ensuring alignment, integration, and delivery across multiple corporate initiatives.

title: Program Manager
description:
Do you want to be part of a mission driven organization shaping Canada’s innovation future? Do you thrive in collaborative environments where creativity, continuous improvement, and strategic thinking are valued? Are you motivated by work that strengthens Canada’s competitiveness and delivers meaningful impact for communities and businesses? 

At Mitacs, we take a talent first approach to building Canada’s innovation capacity. We connect researchers with organizations across every region of the country to help solve pressing challenges and accelerate economic growth. By enabling business academic collaboration and supporting the development of highly skilled talent, we contribute to a resilient, future ready Canadian economy. 

Guided by our values — Impact, Integrity, Collaboration, Excellence, and Talent — we work with partners nationwide to advance research, commercialization, and innovation.

Position Summary:
As Mitacs undergoes a multi-year enterprise transformation, we are building the capabilities, systems, and culture to deliver greater impact at scale. We are seeking a highly experienced Program Manager (1-year contract) to lead the orchestration and delivery of programs under the Transformation Management Office (TMO) Portfolio, including, but not limited to, Mitacs’ Enterprise Transformation (MET).

Reporting to the Director, Enterprise Transformation, this role is accountable for ensuring alignment, integration, and delivery across multiple corporate initiatives.

title: Program Manager / Gestionnaire de programme
description:
Vous souhaitez faire partie d’une organisation propulsée par sa mission qui façonne le futur de l’innovation au Canada? Vous aimez évoluer dans des milieux collaboratifs où la créativité, l’amélioration continue et la pensée stratégique sont valorisées? Êtes‑vous une personne motivée par des responsabilités qui contribuent à accroître la compétitivité du Canada et à produire des résultats concrets pour les communautés et les entreprises? 

Pour développer la capacité d’innovation du Canada, à Mitacs, nous misons sur le talent. Nous établissons des liens entre les chercheurs et chercheuses et les organisations dans toutes les régions du pays pour aider à résoudre des défis pressants et accélérer la croissance économique. En facilitant la collaboration entre le secteur privé et le milieu postsecondaire et en appuyant le perfectionnement de talents hautement qualifiés, nous contribuons à une économique canadienne résiliente et capable de relever les défis de demain.

Guidé·es par nos valeurs, impact, intégrité, collaboration, excellence et talent, nous collaborons avec des partenaires de partout au pays pour faire avancer la recherche, la commercialisation et l’innovation. 

Description du poste :
Nous sommes à la recherche d’un ou d’une gestionnaire de programme pour diriger la planification et la mise en œuvre de programmes du Bureau de gestion de la transformation, y compris, sans s’y limiter, la transformation organisationnelle de Mitacs (MET).

Built for perpetuity

So we decided to build the opposite:

A stable business without external funding

College of the North Atlantic (CNA) is committed to providing an inclusive learning environment which celebrates and encourages diversity. CNA promotes equal access and opportunities to facilitate success in the educational community and in the community at large.

CNA strives to create an organizational culture that welcomes and amplifies diverse perspectives, nurtures belonging and actively seeks to eliminate barriers to equity. We invite all interested individuals to apply, and as part of our shared commitment to the advancement of equity, diversity and inclusion (EDI) as a true reflection of CNA’s core values (Excellence, Integrity, Diversity, Respect and Transparency), we encourage applications from equity-seeking groups including, but not limited to, Indigenous Peoples, racialized persons, persons with disabilities, those who identify as women, and members of 2SLGBTQIA+ communities.

For further information about a career at the Office of the Auditor General, please contact our Manager of People and Culture, Andrea Milley at andreamilley@oag.nl.ca or call 709-725-3351.

The Office of the Auditor General values diversity in the workplace and is an equal-opportunity employer.

Disability-related accommodations and alternate formats related to competitions are available on request by contacting 709-725-3351.

Preference will be given to applicants who are Canadian citizens or permanent residents of Canada, or those who are legally entitled to work in Canada.

Competitions posted may be used to fill future similar vacancies with the Office of the Auditor General.

How to Apply:

Applications must be received on or before the closing date stated for a job posting. It is the responsibility of the applicant to submit an application that demonstrates the required merit criteria. All applications must contain accurate contact information, including current mailing address, email address and phone number. All information submitted as part of an application must be factual, complete and current to the date of submission. Applicants must submit by email to careers@oag.nl.ca

Applications should be consolidated into a single PDF document

Civilian Opportunities

The RNC has roughly 100 civilian employees who play an important role in the delivery of police services every day. Civilians are employed in a wide variety of disciplines including finance, administration, planning, communication, information management and technology, legal services, as well as asset and facilities management.

Civilian employment opportunities available within the RNC are advertised through the Public Service Commission, Government of Newfoundland and Labrador. Visit https://www.hiring.gov.nl.ca to view current job postings.
