title: Reporting & Analytics Business Process Owner (Two Year Term)
description: Venor is proud to partner with the Nova Scotia Liquor Corporation (NSLC) on their search for a Reporting & Analytics Business Process Owner to join their team in Halifax, Nova Scotia. The Nova Scotia Liquor Corporation (NSLC) plays an important role in the province, responsibly managing the sale of beverage alcohol and cannabis while investing back into the communities it serves. Through innovation, operational excellence, and a commitment to public service, the NSLC continues to modernize its business and technology landscape to better serve Nova Scotians. As part of this transformation, NSLC is embarking on a significant SAP modernization initiative and is seeking a Reporting & Analytics Business Process Owner (BPO) to help shape the future of enterprise reporting and analytics. This two-year term opportunity offers the chance to play a pivotal role in one of the organization's most strategic transformation programs, ensuring data, reporting, and business intelligence capabilities are designed to support informed decision-making across the enterprise. Reporting to the Director, Insights & Strategy, this role will serve as the primary business owner for reporting strategy, analytics alignment, and enterprise data definitions while partnering closely with business leaders, project teams, and technology stakeholders throughout the SAP implementation. The Opportunity This role sits at the intersection of business strategy, analytics, and enterprise transformation. Acting as the

title: Senior Structural Engineer
location: St John's, Nova Scotia, New Brunswick, Newfoundland & Labrador, Ontario, CA
description: Senior Structural Engineer  Venor is proud to partner with Tiller Engineering Inc. in their search for a Senior Structural Engineer.  At Tiller Engineering, you'll join a team that has built its reputation on delivering practical, innovative structural engineering solutions for over two decades. Since 2001, Tiller has partnered with clients across the telecommunications, infrastructure, and energy sectors, providing engineering expertise on projects that strengthen communities throughout Atlantic Canada.  As the company continues to grow, they are looking for an experienced Structural Engineer who is eager to take on technically challenging work, mentor others, and play an influential role in shaping projects from concept through completion.  The Opportunity  This is an opportunity to step into a senior technical role where your expertise will directly influence the success of a wide variety of projects. You'll lead structural analysis and design, provide technical oversight, collaborate with multidisciplinary teams, and build trusted relationships with clients and project partners.  You'll enjoy the autonomy to make sound engineering decisions while working alongside experienced professionals who value collaboration, practical thinking, and delivering quality work. Whether you're designing new infrastructure, assessing existing structures, or solving complex engineering challenges, you'll have the opportunity to make a meaningful impact every day.  What You'll Do  As a Senio

title: Dental Faculty Coordinator
description: Venor is proud to partner with Keyin College in the search for a Dental Faculty Coordinator for its Level II Dental Assisting Program.  About Keyin College  Keyin College is one of Newfoundland and Labrador's leading post-secondary institutions, with more than 40,000 graduates since 1980. As the province's largest private college, Keyin delivers career-focused education that equips learners with the practical, in-demand skills needed to succeed in today's workforce.  The Level II Dental Assisting Program reflects this commitment through a nationally approved curriculum that combines classroom learning with hands-on clinical experience in fully equipped dental clinics. This immersive approach equips students with the knowledge, confidence, and real-world skills required to thrive in modern dental practices.  Guided by a collaborative, student-centred culture, Keyin College is committed to educational excellence while fostering the success of its students, faculty, and staff. Joining Keyin means becoming part of an institution dedicated to developing the next generation of skilled healthcare professionals.  The Role  Keyin College is looking for a Dental Faculty Coordinator to lead and support the Level II Dental Assisting Program. This role is responsible for overseeing program delivery, fostering instructional excellence, and ensuring the curriculum continues to meet professional, regulatory, and industry standards. Working closely with instructors, students, and campus leade

title: Senior Sales Engineer
description: Venor is proud to partner with InnovMarine on their search for a Senior Sales Engineer to join their growing team! InnovMarine delivers innovative software solutions and consulting services that help organizations optimize complex asset management operations across the maritime, defence, and industrial sectors. Combining deep technical expertise with industry knowledge, InnovMarine partners with organizations to modernize operations, improve decision-making, and unlock greater value through digital transformation and emerging technologies. As InnovMarine continues to expand across Atlantic Canada, they are seeking a Senior Sales Engineer to help grow their presence within the maritime and defence industries. This unique opportunity blends technical pre-sales expertise with consultative enterprise sales, making it ideal for someone who enjoys translating complex business challenges into practical technology solutions. Reporting directly to the President, you'll play a key role in expanding existing enterprise relationships while developing new business opportunities throughout Atlantic Canada. Working closely with technical and consulting teams, you'll help organizations leverage solutions such as QuickBrain and IBM Maximo to improve asset management, operational performance, and long-term business outcomes. This is a remote position open to candidates residing in Atlantic Canada, with regular travel throughout the region. The Opportunity This role sits at the intersection of

title: Accountant
description: Venor is proud to partner with Archibald Drilling & Blasting in their search for an Accountant to join their team in Truro, Nova Scotia. Archibald Drilling & Blasting is a leading provider of drilling and blasting services, with more than 40 years of experience delivering safe, innovative, and reliable solutions across quarry operations, open-pit mining, civil construction, and marine projects. Known for its commitment to safety, technical excellence, and continuous improvement, Archibald supports some of the most complex and demanding projects throughout Atlantic Canada and beyond. Reporting directly to General Manager, the Accountant will play a critical role in the day-to-day financial operations of the business. This is a highly visible and well-rounded position that will touch all aspects of accounting and finance, while helping leadership navigate cash flow management, operational reporting, and continuous process improvement. The successful candidate will thrive in a fast-paced environment, bring a proactive and solutions-focused mindset, and be motivated by the opportunity to help modernize and improve financial processes as the business continues to grow. What you’ll be doing Managing full-cycle accounting functions across the organization Overseeing accounts payable, accounts receivable, payroll support, reconciliations, journal entries, and general ledger maintenance. Preparing monthly financial statements, reports, and cash flow projections to support operational

title: Director of Operations
description: Venor is proud to partner with Laura Gatien & Associates in their search for a Director of Operations to join their team. They are seeking a people-centric leader who can bring structure, clarity, and steady leadership while championing the values that make LGA unique. About Laura Gatien & Associates Laura Gatien & Associates is a growing counselling practice dedicated to providing accessible, client-focused mental health support within the community. Guided by compassion, professionalism, and a deep commitment to social impact, LGA supports individuals and families through evidence-based care and a welcoming environment. Their work is shaped by five core values:  Warmth, Dedication, Vulnerability, Equality, and Growth, which inform how they care for clients, collaborate as a team, and show up for their community. This role is a strong fit for an experienced operations leader who enjoys working alongside a dynamic entrepreneur to bring ideas to life and turn vision into execution. The Director of Operations will serve as the CEO’s essential complement, harmonizing the major functions of the business, fostering accountability, strengthening culture, and driving sustainable traction. What Success Looks Like This is not a role where you'll be expected to take the reins on day one. Instead, you'll benefit from a thoughtful, phased onboarding designed to set you up for long-term success. Working alongside the  Owner, you'll spend your first 6-12 months learning every aspect of the

title: Senior Platform Engineer
description: Venor is proud to partner with QuickFacts on their search for a Senior Platform Engineer to join their growing team! QuickFacts is a Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) company transforming how insurance professionals access and manage information. Their platform brings together underwriting guidelines, carrier documentation, workflows, training resources, and comparison tools into a single, searchable experience that saves clients valuable time and improves decision-making. As QuickFacts continues to expand its AI-powered platform, they are seeking a seasoned backend engineer who thrives on building scalable systems, solving complex technical challenges, and developing innovative solutions that deliver meaningful business impact. This is an opportunity to join a growing technology company where your work will directly influence product strategy, platform architecture, and the future of AI-driven information management within the insurance industry. The Opportunity As a Senior Platform Engineer, you will play a critical role in designing and scaling the services, infrastructure, APIs, and data systems that power QuickFacts' next generation of products. This position sits at the intersection of software engineering, large-scale information systems, and applied AI. You'll work closely with Product, Front-End Engineering, and DevOps teams to transform complex insurance data into intelligent customer experiences. From designing scalable microservices and data pipelines to optimizing AI-

title: Senior Project Manager
description: Our client, Nexco Construction Company is growing and seeking an ambitious and results-driven Senior Project Manager based in Dartmouth, Nova Scotia. This is an opportunity for a proven construction leader who thrives on ownership, enjoys solving complex challenges, and wants to play a key role in delivering impactful civil infrastructure projects.  About the Role  As a Senior Project Manager, you will lead projects from pre-construction through closeout, overseeing all aspects of planning, execution, financial performance, and client management. Working closely with operations, field leadership, subcontractors, engineers, and clients, you will be empowered to drive project success while contributing to the continued growth and evolution of the organization. We are looking for a leader who is proactive, highly accountable, and motivated by delivering exceptional results in a fast-paced, entrepreneurial environment.  Key Responsibilities  Lead the successful delivery of civil construction projects from pre-construction through completion and closeout.   Develop, manage, and maintain project schedules, budgets, forecasts, and resource plans.   Oversee project financial performance, identifying risks and opportunities to maximize profitability.   Coordinate and manage subcontractors, suppliers, consultants, and internal project teams.   Ensure projects are executed safely, efficiently, and in accordance with contract requirements and quality standards.   Build and maintain stron

title: Social Worker
description: Venor is proud to partner with Oromocto First Nation Child and Family Services in their search for a Social Worker to join their team. Oromocto First Nation Child and Family Services (OCFS) is a designated child and family services agency dedicated to serving the children and families of Oromocto First Nation. Grounded in community, culture, and collaboration, OCFS works to keep children safe, strengthen families, and ensure children remain connected to their identity, traditions, and support systems. Unlike larger agencies, OCFS serves a single community, allowing for smaller caseloads and a more personalized approach to care. Team members work closely together, creating an environment where social workers can devote meaningful time and attention to each child and family while receiving strong organizational support. Reporting to the Director of OCFS, the Social Worker will play a critical role in promoting the safety, wellbeing, and long-term success of children and families within the community. This is a full-time, on-site opportunity based in Oromocto First Nation, New Brunswick, with local travel required and participation in a rotating after-hours on-call schedule. What you'll be doing Conduct child protection investigations, risk assessments, and safety assessments to support the wellbeing of children and families. Develop and manage case plans focused on family preservation, reunification, and kinship or community-based care. Support families navigating complex chall

title: Branch Manager
description: Our client, Graybar is seeking a dynamic and results-driven Branch Manager to lead and grow the branch operations in Kentville, Nova Scotia. This role is ideal for a strategic leader who thrives on developing business opportunities, driving operational excellence, and building high-performing teams. Reporting to the Regional Vice President, the Branch Manager, you will have full accountability for branch performance—balancing sales growth, customer satisfaction, financial management, and employee development—while acting as a key ambassador of the company in the local market. What You’ll Do Leadership & Strategy Provide strategic direction and lead the execution of branch initiatives aligned with company objectives. Champion a culture of accountability, integrity, collaboration, and continuous improvement. Act as a role model by consistently following company policies, procedures, and values. Sales & Customer Growth Maintain and grow sales volume while strengthening customer relationships. Identify and develop new business opportunities, products, and customers. Build strong relationships with customers and suppliers through meetings, calls, and industry events. Ensure all reasonable customer requests are handled promptly and professionally. Operations & Financial Management Oversee pricing strategies, inventory management, and inventory turns. Manage accounts receivable, credit risks, and cost controls. Prepare and manage annual, monthly, and weekly operational plans and bud

title: Manager of Corporate Accounting
description: Venor is proud to partner with Graybar Canada on their search for a Manager of Corporate Accounting to join their head office team in Halifax, NS. Graybar Canada is a leader in the distribution of high-quality electrical, automation, and telecommunications products, supporting these operations through supply chain management and logistics services. Graybar Canada operates more than 35 branches across Canada. As one of Canada's largest employee-owned companies, Graybar Canada has the power and stability of a big corporation and the integrity and drive of a neighborhood business. What You’ll Be Doing The Manager of Corporate Accounting will support the Director of Accounting and VP, Finance in managing the day-to-day operations of the finance function for the organization. This is a key leadership role within the finance team, ensuring financial statements are accurate, compliant with accounting standards, and delivered on time. We're looking for a collaborative, hands-on professional who enjoys being involved in the details, isn't afraid to roll up their sleeves, and is eager to learn. Success in this role will come from a combination of technical capability, initiative, adaptability, and a genuine desire to contribute and gain new skills. Key Responsibilities Financial Reporting & Close: Support the complete accounting cycle: journal entries, accruals, prepayments, depreciation, account reconciliations & compiling working papers Support the Controller with monthly, quarterly,

title: Office Administrator & Bookkeeper
description: Venor is proud to partner with Archibald Drilling & Blasting in their search for an Office Administrator & Bookkeeper to join their team in Truro, Nova Scotia. Archibald Drilling & Blasting is a leading provider of drilling and blasting services, with more than 40 years of experience delivering safe, innovative, and reliable solutions across quarry operations, open-pit mining, civil construction, and marine projects. Known for its commitment to safety, technical excellence, and continuous improvement, Archibald supports some of the most complex and demanding projects throughout Atlantic Canada and beyond. As the company continues to grow, Archibald is seeking an organized, detail-oriented, and proactive Office Administrator & Bookkeeper to support day-to-day office operations and financial administration. This is a critical role within the organization, ideal for someone who enjoys variety in their work and thrives in a small business environment where they can make a meaningful impact. Reporting directly to leadership, the successful candidate will oversee office administration while managing core accounting and bookkeeping functions. This position offers the opportunity to help shape and improve internal processes as the company continues to evolve. What You'll Be Doing Manage the day-to-day administrative operations of the office Process payroll accurately and in a timely manner Manage accounts payable and accounts receivable functions Perform general bookkeeping duties an

title: Labour Relations Specialist
description: Venor is proud to partner with J.D. Irving, Limited in their search for a new Labour Relations Specialist to join their Human Resources team. J.D. Irving, Limited (JDI) is a premier North American forestry and operations leader. What began in 1882 as a regional, family-owned enterprise has grown into a dynamic, coast-to-coast group of operating divisions with over 140 years of success. Recognized for building a highly efficient, vertically integrated supply chain, J.D. Irving, Limited delivers high-quality lumber, pulp and paper products, and sustainable forest management across Eastern Canada and the United States. At Grand Lake Timber Sawmill, Chipman, NB, JDI is proud of their strong legacy in sustainable forestry and high-quality wood products. Reporting to the HR Manager, the Labour Relations Specialist will play a key role in supporting positive union-management relations, ensuring compliance with collective agreements, and promoting a culture of fairness and accountability. They will support the business by interpreting and administering the collective agreement, maintaining a productive relationship with the Union, managing the grievance process, providing training and development to supervisors on company policies and labour relations best practices, assisting with progressive discipline practices, resolving complex labour relations issues and conducting workplace investigations in conjunction with Industrial Security Ltd. The ideal candidate has a strong background

title: EDI and API Integration Analyst
description: Venor is proud to partner with Seaboard Armour Transportation and Logistics in their search for an EDI and API Integration Analyst to join their team. Seaboard Armour Transportation and Logistics is a premier North American transportation and logistics leader. What began in 1964 as a regional, family-owned petroleum delivery operation in Cape Breton, Nova Scotia, has grown into a coast-to-coast group of companies with over 60 years of success. Recognized as one of Canada’s Best Managed Companies and an industry Employer of Choice, Seaboard Armour delivers safe, reliable, and innovative bulk transport, freight forwarding, and supply chain solutions across Canada and the United States. As part of their growing Data and Integration team,  the EDI and API Integration Analyst is responsible for the operation, support, documentation, monitoring, onboarding, and continuous improvement of customer and partner data integrations across EDI, API, and file-based exchange methods. This role ensures integration processes are reliable, well-documented, supportable, and aligned to business needs across transportation and logistics operations. As valued members of the expanding Data and Integration team, EDI and API Integration Analysts, along with current team of data integration professionals, collectively manage, support, document, monitor, onboard, and continuously enhance customer and partner data integrations across EDI, API, and file-based exchange methods. Together, the data integrati

title: Power BI Report Developer
description: Venor is proud to partner with Seaboard Armour Transportation and Logistics in their search for a Power BI Report Developer to join their team. Seaboard Armour Transportation and Logistics is a premier North American transportation and logistics leader. What began in 1964 as a regional, family-owned petroleum delivery operation in Cape Breton, Nova Scotia, has grown into a coast-to-coast group of companies with over 60 years of success. Recognized as one of Canada’s Best Managed Companies and an industry Employer of Choice, Seaboard Armour delivers safe, reliable, and innovative bulk transport, freight forwarding, and supply chain solutions across Canada and the United States. As part of their growing Data and Integration team, Seaboard Armour Transportation and Logistics is seeking a Power BI Report Developer to design, develop, validate, and support trusted Power BI reports, dashboards, datasets, and analytics solutions across transportation, logistics, dispatch, billing, finance, safety, maintenance, customer service, and executive performance management. With a strong focus on report quality, performance, documentation, user acceptance testing, and adoption of standardized reporting practices, this role helps improve operational visibility, reduce manual reporting effort, and build a more mature analytics foundation for the business. The BI Report Developer is responsible for designing, developing, testing, validating, deploying, and supporting business intelligence repor

title: Marketing Coordinator
description: Venor is proud to partner with Fidelis Law in their search for a Marketing Coordinator to join their team in Moncton, NB Fidelis Law is a dedicated personal injury firm with offices in Moncton and Edmundston, New Brunswick. The firm is committed to providing exceptional legal representation to personal injury victims while delivering personalized, compassionate service that ensures clients’ rights are protected and their voices are heard. As Fidelis Law continues to grow, they are seeking a Marketing Coordinator to support and execute marketing initiatives that strengthen brand awareness, enhance community presence, and support business development objectives. Reporting to the Partners of the firm, the Marketing Coordinator will play a key role in coordinating campaigns, creating engaging content, and supporting the day-to-day marketing activities that promote the firm's services and values. This is an exciting opportunity for a creative and organized marketing professional who enjoys working in a collaborative environment and contributing to meaningful work that positively impacts clients and communities across New Brunswick. What you'll be doing: Support the planning, coordination, and execution of marketing campaigns across digital, print, social media, and community engagement channels. Assist in establishing marketing plans and objectives that align with the firm's business initiatives and growth strategies. Create and manage content for social media platforms, website u

title: Vice President of Finance
description: Venor is excited to partner with the Nexco Group on their search for a Vice President of Finance to join their team based in Dartmouth, NS. Nexco Group is a proudly Nova Scotian, integrated group of companies delivering trusted solutions across civil construction, traffic control, and infrastructure services. Through its divisions, Nexco Construction, Nexco Traffic Control, and Nexco Environmental, the company plays a vital role in building and maintaining the infrastructure that supports communities across Atlantic Canada. With expertise spanning mass excavation, site development, demolition, road building, highway structures and bridges, retaining walls, and traffic management, Nexco Group is known for its commitment to safety, quality, reliability, and collaboration. Driven by a passion for excellence and a people-first approach, Nexco partners with clients to successfully deliver projects that contribute to Atlantic Canada's continued growth and development. About the Role: We are seeking a highly strategic Vice President of Finance to provide executive-level oversight for the Nexco Group. As the ultimate owner of our financial vision, you will drive big-picture planning, manage complex cash flow and risk, and optimize internal controls to ensure sustainable, profitable growth across Nexco and a subsidiary of companies. Key Responsibilities: Strategic Financial Leadership & Planning Serve as the primary financial visionary for the Nexco Group, aligning financial strategie

title: Supply Chain Manager
description: SureShot Solutions designs, manufactures, and markets the most innovative dispensers of beverages and beverage add-ins, quenching the thirst of anyone looking for a better way to serve refreshments. With many industry firsts, they are passionate about identifying customer needs, and are tenacious about providing breakthrough solutions to address them. Over the years, they have proudly collaborated with top quick-serve restaurant chains and convenience retailers worldwide to design, engineer, manufacture, and market dispensers for beverages and beverage enhancers. At the heart of their success lies a deep-rooted commitment to excellence, where innovation meets unparalleled craftsmanship. Adaptability to change and a commitment to ensuring a safe workplace while delivering top-notch quality results are qualities SureShot holds in high regard. On behalf of SureShot, we are recruiting a Supply Chain Manager to lead key functional areas including procurement, inventory performance management, planning, and scheduling, while providing direct leadership to the Logistics Lead & Scheduler and the Purchasing Agent. Are you a strategic and systems-savvy supply chain professional who thrives on optimizing operations, leading teams, and delivering results? Reporting directly to the Vice President of Finance and Administration, this role is a critical member of the Supply Chain Management (SCM) Team and plays a central role in driving operational efficiency and supplier performance. Amal is an innovative and vibrant organization providing a diverse range of programs and services. We pride ourselves on our creative approaches to meeting the needs of young people, families and communities, and work hard to provide responsive, respectful and effective support and intervention.

We employ individuals in many disciplines, including social work, accounting, therapy, office administration and human resources, with our largest employee group consisting of child and youth care workers. Our workforce is regularly expanding as our organization grows.

If you are enthusiastic, passionate, committed to excellence and willing to work in a Growth Culture, then Amal may be the place for you.

Send your resume and cover letter to careers@amalnl.ca 

ABOUT

The vision of Amal Youth and Family Centre is to become a centre of hope, and a warm, welcoming, respectful and diverse organization in which children, youth and families from myriad backgrounds thrive and succeed together.

JUNIOR MECHANICAL ENGINEER - C-CORE

At C-CORE, we are committed to investing in people and creating a dynamic and rewarding employment experience. We are seeking a Junior Mechanical Engineer to join our Oceans & Energy Team. This position is based in St. John’s, NL.

If you have a passion for complex design solutions, hands-on fabrication, and contributing to cutting-edge R&D projects from concept to commissioning, this opportunity may be for you. Are you enthusiastic about engineering, attentive to detail, and motivated to support a diverse team of professionals in a dynamic environment? If so, we want you on our team!

About C-CORE

C-CORE conducts R&D and provides research-based advisory services, delivering innovative engineering and technology solutions to national and international clients. For over 50 years, we have built a world-class team of professionals in Remote Sensing and Oceans & Energy. We are global leaders in Earth Observation and geospatial intelligence, with our flagship platform Coresight driving major contracts in defense, environmental monitoring, and advanced R&D.

About Wedgwood Insurance

At Wedgwood Insurance, our core values of We Care, We Lead, We Improve, and We Deliver are reflected in everything we do. It’s how we’ve become the most trusted insurance broker in Newfoundland & Labrador and have been part of our foundation for over 45 years. We invest in our team by promoting personal and professional development, providing amazing benefits, and fostering a culture of respect and pride in our work. And it shows as we’ve been named one of the Best Places to Work in Atlantic Canada by Atlantic Business Magazine!

We are looking for a System Specialist to join our St. John’s office.

Our Career Opportunity – System Specialist

This isn’t a support desk role. As Systems Specialist, you are the person who keeps the engine running. You’ll own the operation, maintenance, and optimization of our brokerage management system – primarily Applied EPIC – along with the supporting platforms our team depends on every day.

Office Clerk | Moncton, NB



Join a team dedicated to delivering outstanding client service.

We hire self-motivated, team-minded individuals with a strong work ethic, integrity and a commitment to client service.

Our Moncton office has an immediate opening for an Office Clerk. Position is permanent, full-time.

Managing, ordering, and stocking of all office supplies;
Basic maintenance of photocopiers and the postage machine;

Depositing outgoing mail at end of day.

As Director, Lifecycle & CRM, you will lead the strategy and execution of how we communicate with clients across email, SMS, and owned digital channels. You will own lifecycle strategy, campaign development, and optimization—establishing the technology configuration and operational model that will scale with RBCx's growth. You will lead a team of 12 specialists, connecting their individual disciplines into a single, cohesive practice.

title: Growth & Merchandising Associate
description: About UsPropel builds technology that strengthens the social safety net in America. We envision a future where every American has the tools and resources they need to thrive.The safety net’s strength depends not just on funding, but delivery. Propel exists to dramatically improve how government benefits are delivered. Managing SNAP benefits, picking a D-SNP plan, or protecting against EBT theft should feel as seamless as Apple Pay or Netflix.Governments struggle to build modern, scalable user experiences. We don’t.Over 5 million Americans trust Propel today. We’ve grown revenue 35% YoY and are reinventing entire benefit categories, including Medicare, around the real needs of low-income Americans. We’re building a high-growth, high-ceiling, sustainable for-profit company where impact drives revenue.The RoleWe're looking for a results-oriented Growth & Merchandising Associate to own the operational and executional layer of our Growth & Merchandising function — campaign coordination, inventory governance, and enablement materials.The Propel Marketplace connects our ~10 million users — predominantly low-income Americans managing government benefits — to products, services, and offers from leading brands. The work you do here directly shapes what users see, what partners can sell, and how the Marketplace grows.This role will join at a pivotal moment for Propel Marketplace. You'll partner closely with the Growth & Merchandising Lead on direction and priorities, but you'll own how

title: Manager, Corporate Accounting & Reporting
description: Atlantic Corporation Limited (ACL), part of the Shannon Group of Companies, is seeking a Manager, Corporate Accounting & Reporting to join their team in Halifax. This is a unique opportunity for a CPA who is looking to move beyond a traditional accounting role and gain exposure to a broad portfolio of privately held businesses spanning transportation, healthcare, real estate, construction, information technology, and more. From industry-leading brands such as Seaboard Transport and Shannex to an extensive real estate portfolio, ACL serves as the corporate office supporting a diverse and growing group of entrepreneurial companies. The ACL Finance team sits at a unique intersection of operations, governance, executive leadership, shareholders, and the Board of Directors - providing both strategic support and critical oversight across the organization. For the right person, this role offers something few accounting opportunities can: a front-row seat to how successful businesses operate, grow, acquire, and evolve. Few organizations can offer the breadth of experience and exposure available through ACL. This team helps shape the future of one of Atlantic Canada's most successful privately held business groups. There truly is no other experience like it in Nova Scotia! This is a rare opportunity to see an entire business from the centre of it. The Opportunity: Reporting to the Senior Director, Finance & Accounting at ACL, with a dotted-line relationship to the Director of Finance

title: Senior Software Developer
description: Venor is proud to partner with Opus Fund Services on their search for a Senior Software Developer to join their Innovation & Product Support (IPS) team! Opus Fund Services is a global provider of fund administration, investor services, and middle-office solutions, delivering innovative technology and exceptional client service to investment managers around the world. Their Innovation & Product Support team is focused on designing, developing, and continuously enhancing the technology that powers both internal operations and client-facing solutions. As part of this collaborative and forward-thinking team, you'll have the opportunity to work on modernizing existing applications while helping shape the next generation of products through emerging technologies, AI-assisted development, and modern engineering practices. This is an excellent opportunity for an experienced software developer who enjoys solving complex technical challenges, writing high-quality code, and working across both modern and legacy applications within a collaborative development environment. The Opportunity As a Senior Software Developer, you'll play a key role in designing, developing, and maintaining applications across the Opus technology ecosystem. This is a hands-on development role where you'll contribute throughout the entire software development lifecycle, from solution design through deployment and ongoing support. Working closely with Product Managers and fellow developers, you'll help build scala

title: General Manager
description: About the Opportunity Our client, GT Painting, is a well-established and highly respected commercial and residential painting contractor with a strong reputation for delivering quality work, exceptional customer service, and long-term client relationships across Atlantic Canada. Having built a successful business with an entrepreneurial mindset, the company is entering its next phase of growth. With an established operation in Nova Scotia and a recently expanded presence in New Brunswick, the business is seeking a General Manager to partner closely with the Owner to help scale the organization, strengthen operations, and build the foundation for continued growth. This is more than a traditional operations role. It is an opportunity to become the operational leader of a growing company—someone who thrives on building teams, improving processes, driving accountability, and creating sustainable growth. The ideal candidate is an ambitious, high-energy leader who wants to help build something meaningful and leave a lasting impact on the business. The Opportunity Reporting directly to the Owner, the General Manager will assume full operational leadership of the business with accountability for profitability, operational performance, team leadership, and strategic execution. As the operational counterpart to the Owner, you will translate vision into execution by ensuring projects, people, systems, and financial performance are aligned to achieve the company's long-term objectives. T

title: Branch Manager (Moncton)
description: Venor is proud to partner with Graybar Canada in their search for a Branch Manager to join their team in Moncton, NB.  Graybar Canada is a leader in the distribution of high-quality electrical, automation, and telecommunications products, supporting these operations through supply chain management and logistics services. Graybar Canada operates more than 35 branches across Canada. As one of Canada’s largest employee-owned companies, Graybar Canada has the power and stability of a big corporation and the integrity and drive of a neighborhood business.  Reporting to the Regional Vice President, the Branch Manager will lead a team of 20 + experienced and professional sales staff, providing steady management, tracking large project opportunities, and acting as the key liaison between Head Office and the branch. This is an excellent opportunity for an experienced, hands-on manager to step into a well-established, high-performing team and build on its success.  What You'll Be Doing  Managing and supporting a team of 20+ experienced sales professionals, providing steady, hands-on leadership.  Maintaining sales volume and customer relations while managing new products and customers.  Determining merchandise and services to be sold and implementing pricing policies.  Managing accounts receivable aging and credit risk.  Overseeing inventory, including inventory turns and slow-moving, defective, or obsolete stock.  Monitoring branch costs and expenditures in line with Head Office proced

title: Human Resources Business Partner (HRBP)
description: Who is COVE? COVE is a global technology partner advancing high-impact solutions from seabed to space. COVE transforms innovation into market-ready technologies through testing, commercialization support, market expertise and access to critical infrastructure. Leveraging its networks and mission-driven platform, COVE connects investors, innovators, industry, and the research community to drive cutting-edge capabilities for commercial and defence applications. Based in Canada and operating internationally, COVE supports the full lifecycle of maritime and multi-domain technologies – from research and development to manufacturing, integration, and export – linking Canadian innovation to global markets. COVE has built a globally recognized innovation platform, with an ecosystem of more than 700 organizations spanning startups, multinational firms, researchers, Indigenous partners, and public sector collaborators. Through its programs, facilities, and partnerships, COVE enables real-world testing and validation, strengthens supply chains, and accelerates technology adoption in complex operating environments. With strong connections across the North Atlantic, the United States, and the Arctic, and as part of international networks including NATO’s Defence Innovation Accelerator for the North Atlantic (DIANA), COVE plays a key role in advancing technologies that support economic growth, environmental sustainability, and global security. As COVE experiences rapid expansion, it is act

title: Senior Full Stack Developer
description:
Senior Full Stack Developer
Canada, Remote
Apply

About CoLab

At CoLab, we help mechanical engineering teams bring life-changing products to market years sooner.

CoLab is the AI platform for driving stronger engineering decisions. Every design review in CoLab builds a knowledge repository of design feedback, decisions, and lessons learned - which AI agents draw from to flag issues on future designs before they compound. The more your team works in CoLab, the smarter it gets and the faster you arrive at the ideal design. Companies like Ford, Komatsu, and Johnson Controls use CoLab to catch issues earlier, eliminate rework cycles, and bring products to market faster.

Founded in St. John’s, Newfoundland, CoLab has grown quickly from our first customer in 2019 to a rapidly scaling company. We’ve recently been recognized on Deloitte’s Fast 50™ and Fast 500™, and named Canadian company to watch by The Globe and Mail and Financial Post. 

About the Role: 

Mechanical engineering teams rely on a complex ecosystem of CAD, PLM, and enterprise systems to design, review, and manufacture products. At CoLab, we're building software that fits naturally into those workflows.

As a Senior Full Stack Developer, you'll play a key role in making that possible. You'll build the integrations and platform capabilities that connect CoLab with the systems our customers use every day, helping engineering organizations review designs faster, collaborate more effectively, and make better decisions th

title: (Senior) Laundry Supervisor | Le Méridien Saigon
description: POSITION SUMMARY   Inspect guest rooms, public areas, pool, etc. after being cleaned by Housekeeper to ensure quality standards. Run sold room reports, verify room status, determine discrepant rooms, prioritize room cleaning, and update status of departing guest rooms. Assist Housekeeping management in managing daily activities. Act as a liaison to coordinate the efforts of Housekeeping, Engineering, Front Office, and Laundry. Document and resolve issues with discrepant rooms with the Front Desk. Prepare, distribute, and communicate changes in room assignments. Communicate issues to next shift. Complete required paperwork.   Assist management in hiring, training, scheduling, evaluating, counseling, disciplining, and motivating and coaching employees. Follow all company and safety and security policies and procedures; report any maintenance problems, safety hazards, accidents, or injuries; complete safety training and certifications. Ensure uniform and personal appearance are clean and professional; maintain confidentiality of proprietary information; protect company assets. Welcome and acknowledge all guests according to company standards; anticipate and address guests’ service needs; assist individuals with disabilities; thank guests with genuine appreciation. Speak with others using clear and professional language. Develop and maintain positive working relationships with others; listen and respond appropriately to the concerns of other employees. SENIOR IT CONTRACT MANAGER (COMPUTER SERVICES OFFICER 4)

About Us
At CSDS, we prioritize people—both within our team and in the communities we serve. Our mission is to redefine how Nova Scotians engage with government by creating accessible, reliable digital solutions centered around user needs.

In today’s tech-driven world, CSDS empowers government departments with essential digital solutions to meet Nova Scotians’ expectations. As a trusted partner across government, we enable departments to fulfill their mandates with secure, user-friendly services. From healthcare and education to public safety, we collaborate to transform how services are designed and delivered.

We don’t just implement technology; we modernize outdated systems and reimagine processes. Our agile, data-driven approach ensures that digital services are both efficient and adaptable to Nova Scotia’s evolving needs.

Joining CSDS means contributing to work that makes a real difference. From providing vital tech support to healthcare professionals to strengthening cybersecurity and developing scalable digital platforms, you’ll play a key role in building a more inclusive, responsive government for Nova Scotians.

Together, we’re setting new standards for digital public service, delivering resilient solutions that meet today’s needs—and anticipate tomorrow’s

Mariner innovates always and everywhere, turning novel ideas into practical solutions. From products and services to venture creation, we build better futures powered by people. title: FastTrackHER Pilot Participant
description:
Skip to content
EMPLOYER SERVICES
CAREER & YOUTH SERVICES
ABOUT
NEWS & EVENTS
CONTACT
CAREER AND YOUTH SERVICES

Equiforce provides innovative, responsive, and industry-informed workforce development initiatives that build equitable pathways into the industries shaping Newfoundland and Labrador’s future and connect women and gender-diverse individuals with opportunity.

CAREER PROGRAMS
FASTTRACKHER

An innovative workforce readiness initiative aimed at preparing women for high-demand industries. FastTrackHER is a new pilot in development for 2026. Short, intensive, and industry-informed, it creates pathways into underrepresented fields through sector connections, relevant certifications, and direct insight into emerging industry opportunities. The first pilot focuses on Newfoundland and Labrador’s construction sector in the Avalon region, with future iterations expanding to other industries across the province.

PATHWAYS TO PLACE

A province-wide pilot launching in 2026 that connects newcomer and immigrant women with eight-week, paid, work-integrated learning placements in local organizations. Participants gain workplace readiness skills, valuable Canadian experience, professional networks, and wraparound supports that promote success and belonging. Employers, in turn, strengthen their capacity through structured guidance that supports inclusive hiring, onboarding, and workplace practices.

For more information and to learn how to participate, click here.  

CAREER PATHWAYS

SUMMARY OF DUTIES

Situated in the heart of North America, the province of Ontario is Canada’s economic powerhouse. It generates nearly 40% of Canada’s GDP, and is home to approximately 40% of Canada’s population. 

The Government of Ontario, Canada operates a network of representational offices – international trade and investment offices (TIOs) in key cities around the world. They are co-located in Canadian diplomatic missions, composed of a Canadian diplomat (head of the TIO) and a locally engaged team of staff.

Salary Range: $68,959 to $79,568
Posting Date: June 9, 2026
Closing Date: Will remain open until filled

This is a full-time position with no fixed end date, subject to external funding.

ACENET is seeking a Research Consultant in Quantum Computing to assist academic and industry clients in understanding, evaluating, and applying emerging quantum computing technologies alongside advanced computing resources (HPC, AI, Cloud) available through ACENET and the Digital Research Alliance of Canada.

This role is focused on enabling early exploration and responsible adoption of quantum computing, rather than hardware development or theoretical research. The successful candidate will have access to quantum computing platforms to support hands-on experimentation and applied projects.

ACENET’s central mission is to help researchers and innovators get their work done more efficiently by providing access to advanced computing resources and making those resources easier to use.

The position builds and maintains strong internal and external referral networks to connect youth with appropriate mental health supports. The incumbent will also manage project data, reporting, and continuous improvement to support effective outcomes and accountability. Additional responsibilities are carried out as required to support the success of the project.

Maintain confidential, trauma-informed, and culturally responsive approaches to working with diverse populations.

Location:                     Corner Brook, NL
Position Type:             1 full-time position (maternity leave coverage) until March 31, 2027, with a possibility of extension pending budget availability and satisfactory performance

Reporting to the Manager of Employment Services, the Career Practitioner is responsible for assisting newcomers to Canada with preparing for, obtaining and maintaining meaningful employment in Newfoundland and Labrador. This role is responsible for overseeing the planning, coordination, delivery, and evaluation of job, employment, and career skills training for newcomer and immigrant clients seeking entry-level employment. The responsibilities also include facilitating labor market engagement, ensuring vulnerable newcomers access employment counseling support, and assisting individuals in navigating various internal and external educational and employment programs. The position aims to expand employment assistance, work readiness support, and entrepreneurship services for newcomers and employers in rural Newfoundland and Labrador, specifically in Corner Brook and the Western region. Senior Mechanical Manager   

Solace Power Inc. is seeking to hire a Senior Mechanical Manager.

Developing wireless power technology isn’t your everyday office job!

The Senior Mechanical Manager will be a key member of the Solace technology department. Reporting to the Senior Director of Product Development, the role will entail working with a cross functional team to deliver solutions spanning production, development, and research projects. The Senior Mechanical Manager will lead Solace’s mechanical department, a team which is integral to all stages of the development process; from early use-case conceptualization and feasibility to product development, production ramp-up, and beyond. Our Approach

Stewart McKelvey has a long history of being a leader in Atlantic Canada. And being a leader means embracing innovation.

We changed the traditional approach to law student recruitment in Atlantic Canada. Typically, Articled Clerks wait for “hire-back” decisions well into their articling year.

Stewart McKelvey is thinking differently.

We believe that success in the practice of law requires making a commitment for more than one summer or one year. Our innovative approach removes the traditional “hire-back” decision-making process, typically done at the end of the articling year. A 2L Summer Student will advance to becoming an Articled Clerk, then directly to an Associate at Stewart McKelvey.

Safely operate the split barge in various weather and sea conditions.

Ensure vessel compliance with maritime safety regulations.

Monitor and maintain proper functioning of propulsion, steering, and all systems.

Lead and manage the crew, ensuring safety protocols and daily tasks are followed.

Proven experience in safety procedures and emergency response.

Expertise in vessel handling and operational control.

Knowledge of preventative maintenance and equipment upkeep.

Strong leadership and communication skills for managing crew.

Excellent verbal and written communication skills.

Strong organizational skills and attention to detail.

Strong analytical, problem-solving, and decision-making skills.

Ability to maintain integrity, professionalism, and confidentiality.

description: Venor is proud to partner with ReelData AI on their search for a Finance & Business Process Analyst to join their growing team in Dartmouth, Nova Scotia. ReelData AI is transforming land-based aquaculture through the application of artificial intelligence, computer vision, and data-driven technology. Their innovative platform helps fish farms optimize feeding, improve fish health, and increase operational efficiency, supporting a more sustainable future for aquaculture around the world. As the company continues to grow, they are seeking a Finance & Business Process Analyst to help build greater operational leverage across finance, reporting, people operations, and internal business processes. This is a highly collaborative role that works closely with the CFO while partnering with leaders across production, deployment, engineering, product, and commercial teams to improve the systems that keep the business running efficiently. This is a hybrid position based out of ReelData AI's Dartmouth office. Candidates must be based in Canada and able to work regularly from the Halifax Regional Municipality. title: Financial Analyst

Champlain Seafood is the largest processor of lobster and snow crab sourced from the Canadian Maritimes and American Northeast. The company has been recognized as one of the Best Managed Companies in Canada and one of the Best Places to work in Atlantic Canada over the last three years. With multiple processing operations and continued growth across the business, the organization is investing further in the financial tools and insights that support day-to-day operations and long-term planning. Working alongside an experienced corporate finance group and a strong plant leadership team, this role will help strengthen data visibility across production, costing, and margins. The successful candidate will help connect plant operations with corporate finance, using strong analytical skills and practical insight to support a growing production platform focused on efficiency, continuous improvement, and long-term success. title: Enterprise Managed Services Leader
description: Putting people first, every day BDO is a firm built on a foundation of positive relationships with our people and our clients. Each day, our professionals provide exceptional service, helping clients with advice and insight they can trust. In turn, we offer an award-winning environment that fosters a people-first culture with a high priority on your personal and professional growth. Your Opportunity BDO Canada is seeking an accomplished Enterprise Managed Services Leader to help lead the next phase of growth for its Managed Services business. Working alongside the existing Managed Services leadership team, this role will be responsible for establishing and scaling BDO's enterprise managed services capabilities, enabling the firm to win, deliver, and grow complex enterprise client relationships. This leader will combine business development, operational leadership, and client executive engagement to build a differentiated enterprise managed services practice. The role is accountable for leading enterprise pursuits, overseeing strategic client portfolios, developing scalable service offerings, maturing delivery capabilities, building high-performing teams, and driving profitable growth. Equally important, the successful candidate will help transform the practice from a transactional delivery organization into one focused on long-term client outcomes, innovation, and strategic partnerships. BDO has built a successful and growing Managed Services business with a strong reputation

Location: Renews (Renews Depot )

Salary: $20.86- $22.51 per hour (CG-19), 40 hours per week

Benefits: Onboarding

Position Details

Transportation and Infrastructure - Government of Newfoundland and Labrador

This is manual, at times physically demanding, semi-skilled work required in the operations and maintenance of government roads and bridges. Work may involve the safe operation of various tools and equipment used for highway maintenance, including the incidental operation of a motor vehicle. Work is often labour intensive, and may involve heavy lifting. Work is performed both inside and outside, sometimes in adverse weather conditions. Examples of work include pothole patching; ditching; repairs to road signs and guardrails; shovels snow from doorways and walkways; picks up parts and materials; loads and unloads trucks; keeps job sites clean and free of debris; digs fence post holes; cleans and maintains tools and equipment; acts as flag person in the direction of highway traffic, where required. Performs other related duties.

CONTRACTOR STEWARDSHIP: 

Build and maintain relationships with contractors, fabricators, and equipment vendors involved in SO2 Reduction Projects. Ensure they adhere to the quality inspection program and meet all specified requirements.

Write reports summarizing findings and observations at the respective fabrication shops.

Work closely with the Sarnia teams, including the project’s QA/QC lead and site FLS, to ensure readiness to receive modules onsite (e.g., crane prep).

Ensure the quality of newly fabricated equipment meets industry and company standards.

About CoLab

At CoLab, we help mechanical engineering teams bring life-changing products to market years sooner.

CoLab is the AI platform for driving stronger engineering decisions. Every design review in CoLab builds a knowledge repository of design feedback, decisions, and lessons learned - which AI agents draw from to flag issues on future designs before they compound. The more your team works in CoLab, the smarter it gets and the faster you arrive at the ideal design. Companies like Ford, Komatsu, and Johnson Controls use CoLab to catch issues earlier, eliminate rework cycles, and bring products to market faster.
Founded in St. John’s, Newfoundland, CoLab has grown quickly from our first customer in 2019 to a rapidly scaling company. We’ve recently been recognized on Deloitte’s Fast 50™ and Fast 500™, and named a Canadian company to watch by The Globe and Mail and Financial Post.

About the Role

We're looking for a Senior Backend Developer to join our Build Operator team. 

Operator isn't a chatbot bolted onto our product. It's becoming the intelligence layer across CoLab, helping engineering teams search company knowledge, surface design decisions, answer complex questions, and generate insights from years of engineering work.

You'll join the team responsible for the backend systems that make this possible. That means building scalable retrieval pipelines, evolving our AI search infrastructure, integrating structured

Launch your career with a hands-on internship through DeepSense! Open to all current post-secondary students, DeepSense internships connect you with real-world AI and data-driven projects that drive innovation.

By joining our Student Pool, you’ll be considered for internships that align with your background and interests. Throughout the internship, you’ll gain experience, build professional skills, and be supported by experts from the DeepSense team. Many internships lead to employment with project partners, creating a direct path from student learning to professional success.

Before you apply, note the following:

Our student projects are 4 months with only rare exceptions. They are internships/co-ops/work terms.
You must be enrolled in a Canadian academic institution for majority of the project duration.
This is not an application to be hired as DeepSense staff.
Ensure you have AI & ML experience in your resume. Projects may be as relevant as work experience.
We have project partners all over Canada. We will prioritize in-person student-employer pairings.

Through our focus on three key areas of economic growth: start-up and venture capital, scale-up and export, and investment attraction, we’re helping Nova Scotia become an economic success story. Together, we turn local potential into lasting prosperity.

As a provincial crown corporation reporting to the Department of Growth and Development, our team is as innovative and energetic as the clients we serve. We are a client and people-centric organization, and we care deeply about the work we do, guided by our values and our passion to help the companies we work with grow and succeed.

The Investment Associate is an integral member of the Venture Capital Team and is involved in actively seeking and evaluating seed and early stage investment opportunities that offer the best potential for commercial success and financial sustainability. In addition, this role works closely with senior members of the investment team in executing transactions and managing portfolio companies.

PROGRAM MANAGER

About Us
At CSDS, we prioritize people—both within our team and in the communities we serve. Our mission is to redefine how Nova Scotians engage with government by creating accessible, reliable digital solutions centered around user needs.

In today’s tech-driven world, CSDS empowers government departments with essential digital solutions to meet Nova Scotians’ expectations. As a trusted partner across government, we enable departments to fulfill their mandates with secure, user-friendly services. From healthcare and education to public safety, we collaborate to transform how services are designed and delivered.

We don’t just implement technology; we modernize outdated systems and reimagine processes. Our agile, data-driven approach ensures that digital services are both efficient and adaptable to Nova Scotia’s evolving needs.

Joining CSDS means contributing to work that makes a real difference. From providing vital tech support to healthcare professionals to strengthening cybersecurity and developing scalable digital platforms, you’ll play a key role in building a more inclusive, responsive government for Nova Scotians.

Together, we’re setting new standards for digital public service, delivering resilient solutions that meet today’s needs—and anticipate tomorrow’s challenges.

Director of Finance

 

Your next career move starts here!

 

Genoa Design International (Genoa) is hiring a Director of Finance to join our dynamic Finance & Accounting Team in Mount Pearl, Newfoundland.

 

ABOUT US

At Genoa, founded in 1995 and a leader in production design and 3D modelling services for the shipbuilding and offshore industries, we believe success is built by people who are passionate, curious, and committed to excellence. Proudly supporting major initiatives like Canada’s National Shipbuilding Strategy (NSS) and commercial projects across North America, we foster an environment where collaboration drives innovation, learning and development are continuous, and every team member plays a vital role in shaping our future.

 

ABOUT THE OPPORTUNITY

As the Director of Finance, you’ll play a key role in ensuring the accuracy, integrity, and efficiency of Genoa’s financial operations. You will oversee the full accounting cycle, managing day-to-day financial, treasury, and accounting functions, while leading the development of annual budgets and financial reporting. With a strong emphasis on internal controls and operational excellence, you’ll support strategic decision-making and champion continuous improvement across financial practices.

title: Chief Executive Officer
description:
Building on Strength. Leading What's Next.

ABCO Industries Inc. (ABCO) is a diversified custom turnkey engineering and manufacturing company delivering solutions across the marine, industrial, environmental, and food processing sectors. Operating from a 50,000 sq. ft. waterfront facility in Lunenburg, Nova Scotia, ABCO has spent more than 75 years building a reputation for quality craftsmanship, technical expertise, and customer-focused execution. Through a unique combination of engineering, fabrication, integration, and project delivery capabilities, ABCO serves clients locally and internationally; their projects are in over 60 countries worldwide, providing solutions designed for performance, reliability, and long-term value.

Since acquiring the business in 2018, the ownership team has built upon that strong foundation through significant investments in infrastructure, capabilities, and long-term growth initiatives. Today, ABCO is a profitable and growing organization with approximately 60 employees, a highly experienced leadership team, and increasing momentum within Canada's marine sector, where substantial government investment continues to create opportunity.

The Opportunity

With a strong foundation, respected reputation, and growing market presence, ABCO is now seeking its next Chief Executive Officer. Reporting to the ownership group, the CEO will provide strategic and operational leadership across the organization’s portfolio of marine, food processing, industria

Lead the transformation of health care for Islanders. Health PEI is seeking a visionary, collaborative, and results-oriented Chief Executive Officer to guide the organization through its next chapter.

The Company
Health PEI is the provincial health authority responsible for delivering publicly funded health services across Prince Edward Island. Guided by its mission of delivering high-quality, person-centered care to every Islander, Health PEI provides a broad continuum of services, including primary care, acute care, long-term care, mental health and addictions services, public health programs, and community-based care. Health PEI employers over 5000 dedicated staff with an operating budget of approximately $1.2 billion.

Health PEI's 2025–2028 Strategic Plan outlines an ambitious path forward focused on connecting every Islander to primary care, improving access to care that meets or exceeds national standards, advancing a One Island Health System approach, and building a more integrated and connected health system around the needs of Islanders. The organization is guided by the values of accountability, care, collaboration, and excellence. 

The Role

title: Chief Executive Officer
description:
Swept Technologies Inc. (Swept) is transforming how commercial cleaning businesses operate. Founded by industry operators who experienced the challenges of managing frontline teams firsthand, Swept is a leading workforce and operations platform that helps cleaning companies connect their people, streamline operations, and deliver exceptional service. Today, hundreds of organizations across North America rely on Swept's mobile-first technology to manage communication, tasks, quality assurance, training, and workforce performance.

Commercial cleaning represents a vital industry powered by resilient entrepreneurs and dedicated frontline workers. Swept exists to help these organizations operate more effectively, improve service quality, and grow sustainably. The company offers a rare opportunity to create meaningful impact through technology that directly improves the success of small and mid-sized businesses and the livelihoods of the people they employ.

The Opportunity

After successfully navigating a period of transformation, Swept has emerged as a profitable, EBITDA-positive, self-financing SaaS business with a strong product and significant growth potential. The next chapter is focused on accelerating growth, enhancing customer retention, expanding product innovation, and increasing enterprise value.

title: Chief Financial Officer
location: Happy Valley Goose Bay, Newfoundland and Labrador, CA
description:
Chief Financial Officer, Nunatsiavut Group of Companies
Happy Valley-Goose Bay, NL

Join Nunatsiavut Group of Companies (NGC) as Chief Financial Officer and play a key role in supporting the financial strength and day-to-day success of a diverse, community-focused organization. 

The Company

​​​​​​​The Nunatsiavut Group of Companies (NGC), based in Labrador, is dedicated to generating sustainable economic value for Nunatsiavut Beneficiaries. Operating under the Labrador Inuit Capital Strategy Trust (LICST), NGC is a leader across sectors including marine services, aviation, logistics, construction, mining, and real estate. Since 2011, NGC has continued to build on its foundation as Nunatsiavut’s economic development corporation, with a focus on steady growth, sound financial management, and long-term community benefit. 

The Role

​​​​​​​Reporting to the President & CEO and working closely with the Chief Operating Officer, the CFO will play a key role in ensuring strong financial oversight across a diverse group of operating companies. This role is well suited to someone who enjoys a hands-on environment and takes pride in building strong financial processes, producing reliable reporting, and supporting day-to-day business operations. The successful candidate will contribute to a stable and well-run organization while helping ensure financial discipline and accountability across its business units.

title: Vice President, Clinical and Service Excellence
description:
Youthdale Treatment Centres (Youthdale) is a non-profit charitable organization located in Toronto that has been providing compassionate, professional, and collaborative care to children, youth, emerging adults, and families for over 50 years. Accredited with Exemplary Standing by Accreditation Canada, Youthdale serves approximately 3,500 clients each year through a comprehensive range of services, including crisis intervention, live-in treatment, secure treatment, day treatment, outpatient services, and community-based supports.

With a strong commitment to equity, diversity, inclusion, and belonging, Youthdale delivers trauma-informed, evidence-based, and culturally responsive care aimed at helping young people achieve resilience, stability, and hope. As an organization dedicated to advancing mental health equity, Youthdale collaborates closely with hospitals, schools, community agencies, and government partners to ensure children, youth, and their families have access to the highest quality of care. 

Youthdale is seeking a Vice President, Clinical and Service Excellence (Vice President) who plays a crucial role in shaping organizational strategy, strengthening partnerships within the system, advancing innovation, and ensuring Youthdale remains responsive to emerging trends and priorities in Ontario's child and youth mental health sector. The City of Saint John is Canada's oldest incorporated city and one of Atlantic Canada's most historic and evolving municipalities. Nestled on the Bay of Fundy, Saint John is a vibrant urban centre with a rich heritage, strong industrial foundation, growing multicultural population, and a bold vision for the future.

Guided by its mission to "Grow the City. Serve the City. Be the city of choice," the City is committed to delivering exceptional services, fostering growth and inclusion, and creating a thriving community for residents and businesses alike. The City employs more than 600 permanent staff who work together across six service areas to provide essential municipal services and support continued community growth.

As part of a newly designed executive leadership structure, the City is creating greater alignment across services and strengthening its ability to deliver high-quality, sustainable, and efficient municipal operations for the benefit of residents and businesses.

NL2 Capital Inc. is a publicly listed company on the TSX Venture Exchange that was established to identify and complete a business combination with a high-quality operating platform. Through its transformative Qualifying Transaction with the Talon Group, NL2 is evolving from a capital pool company into a fully operating public entity, expected to operate as Talon Diversified Holdings Inc. upon closing.

The Talon Group is a Newfoundland and Labrador-based industrial services platform comprised of established operating businesses including Talon Energy Services, Basil Fearn (93) Limited, and Trinity Roofing. Together, these companies serve industrial, commercial, infrastructure, marine, energy, mining, transportation, public sector, and construction markets throughout Atlantic Canada.

In a strong business position, the organization enters this next chapter with an established operating foundation, demonstrated profitability, and significant growth potential. By transitioning to a public company, Talon Diversified Holdings Inc. will gain enhanced access to capital, enabling it to pursue larger-scale industrial and infrastructure opportunities and accelerate its growth strategy through both organic expansion and strategic acquisitions. With strong fundamentals and exposure to sectors experiencing sustained investment and development, the organization is entering a period of considerable opportunity.

title: Vice-President, Strategic Growth, Productivity & Innovation
description:
Humber Polytechnic is seeking a visionary leader to serve as their Vice-President, Strategic Growth, Productivity & Innovation (VP, SGPI).

This is a rare opportunity to shape the future of a leading Canadian polytechnic while influencing the evolving role of post-secondary education in driving economic growth, productivity, and social impact. Operating at the centre of Humber Polytechnic’s strategic agenda, the VP, SGPI will be uniquely positioned to design and lead an integrated growth model that brings together education, industry, government, and global partners in new and transformative ways.

At a time of profound disruption across higher education, driven by changing learner expectations, technological advancement, and shifting economic conditions, this role offers the chance to build something fundamentally new. The VP will architect Humber’s next-generation growth engine, redefining how institutions expand access, diversify revenue, and deliver lifelong learning at scale. Central to this mandate is the ability to drive measurable growth in revenue through the development of new market opportunities, innovative partnership models, and scalable education and service offerings, including those delivered through complex domestic and international collaborations and contractual arrangements. Through this work, the VP will extend Humber’s reach and impact while positioning the institution as a national leader in productivity and a global innovator in polytechnic education.

title: Vice-President, University Relations
description:
First Nations University of Canada (FNUniv) invites applications for the role of Vice President, University Relations. This role is central to amplifying FNUniv’s story, strengthening institutional reputation, advancing fundraising and revenue diversification, and building respectful, reciprocal relationships with Indigenous and non-Indigenous communities and partners.

About First Nations University of Canada

First Nations University of Canada (FNUniv) is a national leader in Indigenous-led post-secondary education. FNUniv is committed to transformative education rooted in Indigenous Knowledge. With four campuses across Saskatchewan, the university provides a learning environment that integrates Indigenous perspectives, cultures, and histories while preparing students for success. As a federated college of the University of Regina, FNUniv offers undergraduate and graduate programs in business, humanities, social sciences, and sciences—all incorporating Indigenous worldviews, traditions, and languages.

Founded in 1976 as the Saskatchewan Indian Federated College (SIFC), FNUniv was renamed in 2003, reinforcing its commitment to Indigenous-led post-secondary education. FNUniv was created so Indigenous learners could study in places where they feel a genuine sense of belonging — where our Indigenous knowledges, languages, and worldviews guide teaching, learning, and research. title: Chief Administrative Officer
description:
Located along the scenic Saint John River in southern New Brunswick, the Town of Grand Bay-Westfield is a growing and vibrant community recognized for its strong quality of life, natural beauty, and close-knit, family-oriented character. Situated just outside the City of Saint John, the Town offers its 6,000 residents and businesses the benefits of a welcoming small-town environment along with strong regional connectivity and access to urban amenities. Formed through the amalgamation of the former communities of Grand Bay and Westfield, the Town has evolved into a progressive municipality focused on sustainable growth, responsible governance, and community vitality.

The Town of Grand Bay – Westfield is seeking a Chief Administrative Officer (CAO). This position serves as the senior administrative leader of the Town and is accountable for the effective and efficient management of all municipal operations. The CAO provides objective and strategic advice to Council and ensures the implementation of policies, priorities, and programs in alignment with legislative and governance frameworks as well as the strategic plan.

Overseeing all municipal departments, the CAO leads a collaborative and well-established leadership team. The organization is characterized by a strong organizational structure, sound governance, a corporate culture that is well appreciated and has an appetite for growth. description:
The University of Windsor is seeking an experienced and values-driven leader to serve as its Vice-President, Finance and Operations (VPFO). This current vacancy is a pivotal opportunity to shape the future of the University through strategic, innovative, and collaborative leadership in support of UWindsor’s academic mission and long-term success.

The Organization

The University of Windsor is situated on the traditional territory of the Three Fires Confederacy of First Nations: the Ojibwa, the Odawa, and the Potawatomi.

The University is a comprehensive research and teaching institution with more than 16,000 students. Located in the heart of Canada's Border City, the University of Windsor combines excellence in teaching, research, and professional practice with a commitment to build stronger communities, treat people with respect and dignity, and address pressing societal needs. 

The Role

Reporting to the President and Vice-Chancellor, the Vice-President, Finance and Operations (VPFO) provides strategic, ethical, and people-centred leadership across the University’s administrative and operational portfolios. These include finance, facilities, information technology, ancillary services, environmental sustainability, enterprise risk management, and campus safety.
